questions.question: Bluetooth communicates data using low-power radio waves that are within the frequencies ______ and _______
questions.explanation: The frequency band for Bluetooth communication is set by an international agreement. This frequency band is between 2.402 GHz and 2.480 GHz.

questions.question: In a Ring topology network, the data flows ________
options: away from the tokens on the ring
in a direction where the computers in a network are fully functional
questions.explanation: A circular data path is created in a Ring topology. In this type of topology, the data packets travel from one device to the next until they reach their destination. Each device in this topology is connected to two other devices but they are not interconnected. This is the reason for data to travel in a single direction.

questions.question: FTP server _____________
options: Has single TCP connection for a file transfer
Has UDP connection for file transfer
questions.explanation: FTP server maintains state information of every control connection to keep track of the active and inactive connections in the session. This helps the server decide which connection to terminate, in case the connection is inactive for too long.

questions.question: Default administrative distance of a static route is ________
questions.explanation: 1 is the default administrative distance of Static Route. It is used by routers to select the best path when there are different routes to the same destination. It's used only two different routing protocols are being used.

questions.question: An OSPF router receives an LSA. The router checks its sequence number and finds that this number is higher than the sequence number it already has. Which two tasks does the router perform with the LSA?
options: Sends newer LSU update to source router
Floods the LSA to the other routers
questions.explanation: An OSPF router receives an LSA. If the router checks its sequence number and finds that the number is higher than the sequence number of the LSA that it already has, the router adds it to the database, and then floods the LSA to the other routers.

questions.question: The three architectures of TMN are ____________
options: Centralized architecture, Decentralized architecture, and Hierarchical architecture
Tiered architecture, Peer-to-peer architecture, and Client/Server architecture
Security architecture, Physical architecture, and Logical Layered architecture
Peer-to-peer architecture, Security architecture, and Centralized architecture.
questions.explanation: TMN architecture can be divided into three perspectives and those are Security architecture, Physical architecture, and Logical Layered architecture. Network management architecture consists of a Network Management system (NMS), Network Management protocol, Managed devices, Management agents and Management Information. Tiered architecture, Peer-to-peer architecture, and Client/Server architecture are the three types of Network architecture. Centralized architecture, Decentralized architecture, and Hierarchical architecture are architectures of Database Management System.

questions.question: FTP is built on _____ architecture.
questions.explanation: An FTP connection includes a Server and a Client which wish to share a number of data files. The server can transfer files with multiple clients at the same time while the client communicates with only one server at a time.

questions.question: Size of TCP segment header ranges between ___________
questions.explanation: The size of the header can be 20 bytes at a minimum if there are no options and can go up to 60 bytes at maximum with 40 bytes in the options field. The header contains all the control information required to ensure ordered, error-free and reliable delivery of the segment.

questions.question: The commands, from client to server, and replies, from server to client, are sent across the control connection in ________ bit ASCII format.
questions.explanation: FTP was designed to transmit commands only in English characters that are possible with just 7 bits in ASCII. Even the media has to be converted to ASCII before transmission.

questions.question: What is the main advantage of Port Access Control List?
options: It is implemented on layer 3 devices
It supports outbound and inbound traffic
It can have 2 access lists on a layer 2 interface
It can be configured on Ether-Channel also
questions.explanation: Port access control list can be configured on layer 2 devices. As layer 2 deals with MAC and IP addresses, port access list supports both MAC access list and IP access list. So, a single interface can have 2 access lists in layer 2. Ether-Channels cannot configure port access control lists.

questions.question: Which sublayer of the data link layer performs data link functions that depend upon the type of medium?
questions.explanation: Media access control (MAC) deals with transmission of data packets to and from the network-interface card, and also to and from another remotely shared channel. The MAC sublayer also prevents collision using protocols like CSMA/CD.

questions.question: Which of the following protocol is multi stream oriented?
questions.explanation: Stream control transmission protocol provides the facility to transmit multiple streams for a single connection. That is, a single connection can have multiple ways and block of one way(path) does not affect other streams.

questions.question: A scatternet can have maximum __________
questions.explanation: A scatternet can have maximum of 10 piconets and minimum of 2 piconets. To connect these piconets, a slave node of one piconet may act as a master in a piconet that is part of the scatternet.

questions.question: Header translation uses ___________ to translate an IPv6 address to an IPv4 address.
questions.explanation: A mapped IPv6 address contains the IPv4 address in its last 32-bits and is preceded by 16 1s and 80 0s. It can be used to translate an IPv6 address to an IPv4 address.

questions.question: Why a border gateway protocol (BGP) session is used in between routers?
questions.explanation: Usually BGP uses TCP services while establishing the sessions between BGP routers. A session is more like a connecting link to exchange only the information of routing and these BGP sessions are persistent.

questions.question: Multiplexing provides _________
questions.explanation: Multiplexing helps us to transfer our messages over a shared channel. This brings up the issue of privacy and efficiency. Fortunately, Multiplexing has high efficiency and high privacy when implemented because in the implementation, the transport layer of the OSI network model handles the function of multiplexing through interfaces called ports which provide the required efficiency and privacy.

questions.question: DSL telcos provide which of the following services?
questions.explanation: DSL stands for Digital Subscriber Line and ISP stands for Internet Service Provider. In a Digital Subscriber Line system, the same company which provides phone connection is also an ISP. The internet is provided through the pre-installed telephone lines.

questions.question: In the slow-start algorithm, the size of the congestion window increases __________ until it reaches a threshold.
questions.explanation: In slow-start algorithm, the size of the congestion window increases exponentially until it reaches a threshold. When it reaches the threshold, it stops increasing and continues sending packets through the threshold window thus preventing congestion.

questions.question: Network layer firewall has two sub-categories called ____________
options: bit oriented firewall and byte oriented firewall
questions.explanation: Most network layer firewalls can operate as stateful or stateless firewalls, creating two subcategories of the standard network layer firewall. Stateful firewalls have the advantage of being able to track packets over a period of time for greater analysis and accuracy " but they require more memory and operate more slowly. Stateless firewalls do not analyze past traffic and can be useful for systems where speed is more important than security, or for systems that have very specific and limited needs. For example, a computer that only needs to connect to a particular backup server does not need the extra security of a stateful firewall.

questions.question: An interface between a management agent embedded in a network element and a network management system will be a _____ reference point.
questions.explanation: The function blocks in are connected with interfaces designated by x, q3, qx, and f. These are called TMN service interfaces or simply, TNM interfaces. An interface between a management agent embedded in a network element and a network management system will be a q3 reference point.

questions.question: Which of the following protocols is used in the internet?
questions.explanation: HTTP is used to browse all the websites on the World Wide Web, DHCP is used to allot IPs automatically to the users on the internet, and DNS is used to connect the users to the host servers on the internet based on the Domain Name.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of Bluetooth?
questions.explanation: Bluetooth is a wireless technology used to create a wireless personal area network for data transfer up to a distance of 10 meters. It operates on 2.45 GHz frequency band for transmission.

questions.question: Several techniques can make size of routing table manageable and also handle issues such as __________
questions.explanation: The size of the routing table in the technique must be manageable for the network nodes i.e. it must not be too big. Security of the forwarding packet is the highest priority for a technique and must be high enough so that only authorized senders and receivers can access the packet's content.

questions.question: Traffic in a VPN is NOT ____________
options: Restricted to a single protocol in IPsec
questions.explanation: Traffic in a VPN is not accessible from any unauthorized public networks because it is secured with the masking IP address. This provides the benefit of access to blocked resources to the users.

questions.question: What feature differentiates SNMPv3 from SNMPv1 and SNMPv2?
options: It has improved error handling process
It provides a common medium for network devices to share management information
It has improvements in performance and manager-to-manager communication
questions.explanation: SNMPv1 and SNMPv2 have poor security. Network administrators used Telnet and SSH for security purposes in SNMPv1 and SNMPv2. Later SNMPv3 was developed. It was almost similar to SNMPv1 and SNMPv2 except for its added security features. SNMPv3 mainly focuses on security and administration.

questions.question: How do Storage administrators ensure secure access to storage devices?
options: By putting a physical lock on the storage device
By keeping devices shutdown when not in use
questions.explanation: Zoning is a method in SAN that can be used by a storage administrator to specify who can see what in the SAN. Zoning might complicate the scaling process if the size of the SAN increases.

questions.question: Which of the following is the disadvantage of asymmetric cryptography?
questions.explanation: Public key cryptography cannot deal well with bulk messages. It uses very lengthy keys compared to symmetric key cryptography; this makes the system slow to proceed with bulk messages.

questions.question: Which of the following is a disadvantage of virtual local area network?
questions.explanation: In VLAN hooping an attacker can access all the VLANs. The attacker gets the access by penetrating other VLANs with traffic. There are two ways to do this hooping, they are switch spoofing and double tagging.

questions.question: What is/are the property(s) of a good hash function in case of producing outputs for set of inputs?
options: Apparently random and evenly distributed values
Evenly distributed but not apparently random values
questions.explanation: When a huge set of input blocks are given to a hash function, a good hash function will result in unique random values with no relation between them. At the same all the hash values will be equal that is fixed length.

questions.question: How many octets are dedicated to OUI(Organizationally unique identifier) in in 48 MAC address space?
questions.explanation: OUI is the address that is universally administered and identifies the the device uniquely. Out of six octets, three octets are used to identify the OUI, that to which organization it belongs.

questions.question: Bits can be sent over guided and unguided media as analog signal by ___________
questions.explanation: In analog modulation, digital low frequency baseband signal (digital bit stream) is transmitted over a higher frequency. Whereas in digital modulation the only difference is that the base band signal is of discrete amplitude level. The bits are represented by only two frequency levels, one for high and one for low.

questions.question: A pseudo topology that combines a Star topology with either Ring or Bus topology is known as ___________
questions.explanation: Hubs are used to connect segments of LAN. It is a common connection point for devices present in a network. Star topology is usually combined with Bus topology using Hub as the backbone of Bus topology. Router, Ethernet and Switches are network components and can be introduced in network topologies.

questions.question: The diagram below represents ___________ type of data transmission in LAN.
questions.explanation: The given diagram is that of a broadcast data transmission. There are three different types of data transmission in LAN and those are unicast, multicast, and broadcast. In broadcast, packet is send to all the nodes.

questions.question: Servers handle requests for other domains _______
questions.explanation: Whenever a request is received at server from other domains, it handles this situation by contacting remote DNS server.

questions.question: What is the maximum number of errors that can be detected using two-dimensional parity check code?
questions.explanation: Two-dimensional parity check was introduced to overcome the problem in simple parity check code that it is able to find only odd numbers of errors. Two-dimensional parity arranges the code into columns and rows and add parity bits in each column and row to find errors up to 3.

questions.question: Two machines can use the timestamp request and timestamp replay messages to determine the ___________ needed for an IP datagram to travel between them.
options: Travel time for the next router
questions.explanation: The round-trip time refers to the total time taken combining the time taken for a packet sent from a source to reach a destination and the time taken the acknowledgement sent by the destination to reach the source. The Router sends destination unreachable message if the destination is not found.

questions.question: Data communication system spanning states, countries, or the whole world is ________
questions.explanation: WAN is the abbreviation for Wide Area Network. This network extends over a large geographical area. WANs are used to connect cities, states or even countries. A wireless connection is required to build a WAN. The best example of WAN is the Internet.

questions.question: Bluetooth supports _______
options: both point-to-point connections and point-to-multipoint connection
questions.explanation: In Bluetooth, each slave node communicates with the master of the piconet independently i.e. each master-slave connection is independent. The slave is not allowed to communicate with other slaves directly.

questions.question: Which of the following property of the hash function makes the hash function strong?
questions.explanation: Hash function is referred as weak, when it only satisfies the Efficiency, preimage resistant, second image resistant, fixed length output, variable length input. It is referred as strong if it satisfies the collision resistant along with all of the above.

questions.question: The combination of _________ and __________ is often termed the local address of the local portion of the IP address.
questions.explanation: It is termed as the local address because the address won"t be applicable outside the subnet. Sub networking is implemented for remote sensing in transparent way from that host which is contained in the sub network which called a local operation.

questions.question: SMI and MIB are the two parts of _______ model of SNMP.
questions.explanation: Structured Management Information (SMI) and Management Information Base (MIB) are the two modules of SNMP Information model. It is used for monitoring and controlling network devices across the network.

questions.question: What is a web browser?
options: a program that can display a web page
a program used to view html documents
it enables user to access the resources of internet
questions.explanation: A web browser is an application program that is used to access the World Wide Web resources, applications and websites. Some examples of web browsers are Google Chrome, Internet Explorer and Safari.

questions.question: Transport layer protocols deals with ____________
questions.explanation: Transport layer is 4th layer in TCP/IP model and OSI reference model. It deals with logical communication between process. It is responsible for delivering a message between network host.

questions.question: In OSPF header, which field is used to detect errors in the packet?
questions.explanation: Checksum field is used to detect errors. It makes sure that the data portions that are being sent are all in integrity. It can detect duplicated bits. Once an error is detected, the sender has to re-transmit the data as it won"t receive an acknowledgement.

questions.question: Which of the following is not a phase of virtual circuit network?
questions.explanation: There are three phases in a virtual circuit network: setup, data transfer and teardown phase. There is no termination phase in it.

questions.question: Which of the following is the correct sentence about MAC address?
questions.explanation: IP address is represented in decimals (10.10.25.44), whereas, MAC address is represented in hexadecimal(60-30-74-00-ED-FC). The MAC address has total of 6 octets and each octet has 8 bits that is 2 hexadecimal numbers(48bits).

questions.question: What is the function of a Fault Manager (FM)?
options: It receives notifications of events in Network Elements
It provides additional support for vendor specific MIB modules
It is responsible for automatically detecting the presence of new Network Elements in the network
It provides secure access to Network Elements
questions.explanation: The Fault. Manager (FM) receives notifications of events in NEs. It may also infer faults by analysis of data. For instance, by comparing measured throughput. on a link with link capacity, it can detect a congestion fault. The FM notifies the operator through various means such as text, graphics, audio, etc. It may also take automatic corrective action to resolve a fault.

questions.question: In Three-Way Handshaking process, the situation where both the TCP's issue an active open is ___________
questions.explanation: In simultaneous open situation, two nodes send an SYN signal to each other and start a TCP connection. Here, both TCP nodes transmit a SYNC+ACK segment to each other and a connection is established between them. This doesn"t happen usually, because both sides have to know which port on the other side to send to.

questions.question: The underlying Transport layer protocol used by SMTP is ________
questions.explanation: TCP is a reliable protocol, and Reliability is a mandatory requirement in e-mail transmission using SMTP.

questions.question: Which of this is not a network edge device?
questions.explanation: Network edge devices refer to host systems, which can host applications like web browser. A switch can"t operate as a host, but as a central device which can be used to manage network communication.

questions.question: Network addresses are a very important concept of ________
questions.explanation: Network addresses are a very important concept of IP addressing. The first address in a block is used as network address that represents the organization. The network address can be found by AND"ing any address in the block or class by the default mask.

questions.question: Dynamic web page ______
options: is same every time whenever it displays
generates on demand by a program or a request from browser
both is same every time whenever it displays and generates on demand by a program or a request from browser
is different always in a predefined order
questions.explanation: A dynamic web page provides different content every time the user opens it based on some events like new additions or time of the day. Languages such as JavaScript are used to respond to client-side events while languages such as PHP as used to respond to server-side events.

questions.question: For encryption, WiMAX supports _______
options: advanced encryption standard and triple data encryption standard
questions.explanation: Both advanced encryption standard and triple data encryption standard are block cipher techniques and are popularly used in WiMAX and other applications for secure encryption.

questions.question: A repeater is a _________ layer device.
questions.explanation: In an extended network repeaters are used to connect media elements. These repeaters operate on the physical layer of the OSI model. It removes noise from a signal.

questions.question: In satellite WAN ___________ protocol is used.
questions.explanation: CDMA is used in 2G and 3G mobile technologies. CDMA is a channel access method where several transmitters can send their data simultaneously on a single channel. Thus this protocol is used in Satellite WAN.

questions.question: What does each packet contain in a virtual circuit network?
questions.explanation: A short VC number also called as VCID (virtual circuit identifier) is a type of identifier which is used to distinguish between several virtual circuits in a connection oriented circuit switched network. Each virtual circuit is used to transfer data over a larger packet switched network.

questions.question: During error reporting, ICMP always reports error messages to ________
questions.explanation: ICMP notifies the source about the error when an error is detected because the datagram knows information about source and destination IP address. The source can then retransmit the data again or try to correct those errors.

questions.question: SNMPv3 is an internet management standard defined by ________
questions.explanation: IETF (Internet Engineering Task Force) is an open standards organization. It defines Internet Management standards such as SNMPv3.

questions.question: Which of the following is not a similarity between flooding and distance vector routing?
questions.explanation: Both flooding technique and distance vector routing are used to find shortest paths for the destinations. But flooding requires less setup than DVR because, nodes in it need to know information about their neighbors only. And it needs less memory with less setup.

questions.question: PPP consists of ________components
options: Three (encapsulating, a link control protocol, NCP)
Two (a link control protocol, Simple Network Control protocol)
questions.explanation: PPP consists of three components namely Link Control Protocol (LCP), Network Control Protocol (NCP), and Encapsulation. LCP and NCP are the PPP protocols which provide interface for handling the capabilities of the connection/link on the network and encapsulation provides for multiplexing of different network-layer protocols.

questions.question: The maximum size of payload field in ethernet frame is __________
questions.explanation: The minimum size of the payload field is 40 bytes and the maximum size is 1500 bytes. If the payload size exceeds 1500 bytes, the frame is called a jumbo frame.

questions.question: The packet sent by a node to the source to inform it of congestion is called _______
questions.explanation: Choke packet is sent by a node to the source to inform it of congestion. Two choke packet techniques can be used for the operation called hop-by-hop choke packet and source choke packet.

questions.question: Which protocol forbids users from making any modifications like create, delete or rename emails on the mail server?
questions.explanation: POP3 forbids users from making any modifications to the emails on the email server to ensure that no emails are corrupted. IMAP allows such modifications as it can keep up with the modifications. SMTP and MAPI are used for sending emails and providing an interface for programs to make them email aware respectively.

questions.question: What is the constraint on the data per cookie?
questions.explanation: Each cookie can hold up to only 4 KB. In practice, browsers allow many more than 300 cookies total, but the 4 KB size limit may still be enforced by some. Storage of a session has to be a minimum of 5MB.

questions.question: Which of the following hardware in our computer is embedded with MAC address?
questions.explanation: NIC(network interface card) knows only about MAC address which is embedded in it. It doesn"t know about IP address and it is the only hardware in the device which is aware of network communication.

questions.question: Return value of the UDP port "Chargen" is _______
questions.explanation: Using Chargen with UDP on port 19, the server sends a UDP datagram containing a random number of characters every time it receives a datagram from the connecting host. The number of characters is between 0 and 512.

questions.question: In a piconet, one master device ________
options: can be slave in another piconet
can be slave in the same piconet
can be master in another piconet
questions.explanation: In a scatternet, a slave node of one piconet may act as a master in a piconet that is part of the scatternet. The scatternet uses this property to connect many piconets together to create a larger network.

questions.question: In Subscriber loop section _____________ forms a part of active links.
questions.explanation: A WAN is partitioned into two sections based on network management grounds. Subscriber loop Section is one of the two partitions. This section is further classified as passive links and active links. A dedicated wire from central office to customer premises is a part of passive links and coaxial cable is a part of an active link.

questions.question: What is SDH?
options: sdh is similar standard to SONET developed by ITU-T
sdh stands for synchronous digital hierarchy and is a similar standard to SONET developed by ITU-T
questions.explanation: SDH is a standard that allows low bit rates to be combined into high-rate data streams and as it is synchronous, each individual bit stream can be embedded into and extracted from high-rate data streams easily.

questions.question: Which method(s) can be used to establish communication between two different virtual area networks?
options: TDM, frame tagging and table maintenance
questions.explanation: Communication between two virtual area networks can be possible using a router or backbone switch. When back bone switch is used, then any one of the three methods can be used to establish communication between different VLANs.

questions.question: The Cookie manipulation is done using which property?
questions.explanation: The cookie property sets or returns all name/value pairs of cookies in the current document. There are no methods involved: cookies are queried, set, and deleted by reading and writing the cookie property of the Document object using specially formatted strings.

questions.question: Which aspect of the random-access protocols makes them contention methods?
questions.explanation: Random access protocol also called as contention methods have no rules or time for stations to place their frames on the medium. For this reason, stations compete among themselves for accessing the medium.

questions.question: Which of the following architecture uses the CSMA/CD access method?
questions.explanation: Collision detection is not possible in Ethernet without extensions. Collision detection techniques for multiple access like CSMA/CD are used to detect collisions in the Ethernet architecture.

questions.question: Voice privacy in GSM cellular telephone protocol is provided by _______
questions.explanation: The A5/2 cipher was published in the year 1996 and was cryptanalysed in the same year within a month. It's use was discontinued from the year 2006 as it was really weak.

questions.question: In the network HTTP resources are located by
questions.explanation: The Uniform Resource Identifier is a name and locator for the resource to be located by the HTTP. The URLs and URNs are derived through the identifier.

questions.question: What is the minimum hamming distance between the words 0001, 0100, 0110, 1111?
questions.explanation: In order to find the minimum hamming distance between the set of words, the hamming distance between all pairs of the set should be calculated. The least number in the resulted hamming distances will be the minimum hamming distance.

questions.question: The password is sent to the server using ________ command.
questions.explanation: The PASS command, preceded by the username, completes the user's identification for access control in an FTP session. Without the valid password, the user won"t be able to initiate the FTP connection.

questions.question: Among the optical-distribution architectures that are essentially switched ethernet is __________
questions.explanation: AON stands for Active optical networks which are essentially switched Ethernets. Each user has his/her own dedicated optical fiber line connecting to the ISP in an AON.

questions.question: Which layer provides the services to user?
questions.explanation: In networking, a user mainly interacts with application layer to create and send information to other computer or network. Application layer provides the interface between applications and the network. It is the top-most layer in both the TCP/IP and the OSI model.

questions.question: EIGRP sends a hello message after every ___________ seconds.
questions.explanation: EIGRP routers broadcast the hello packets frequently to familiarize with the neighbors. EIGRP routers send the hello message after every 5 seconds on LAN, and every 60 seconds on WAN.

questions.question: Fiber optics posses following properties __________
questions.explanation: In fibre optics the transmission of information is in the form of light or photons. Due to all above properties mentioned in options fibre optics can be submerged in water and are used at more risk environments.

questions.question: If you wanted to have 12 subnets with a Class C network ID, which subnet mask would you use?
questions.explanation: If you have eight networks and each requires 10 hosts, you would use the Class C mask of 255.255.255.240. Why? Because 240 in binary is 11110000, which means you have four subnet bits and four host bits. Using our math, we"d get the following: 24-2=14 subnets 24-2=14 hosts.

questions.question: Next-Hop Method is used to reduce contents of a _________
questions.explanation: In the next-hop forwarding method, the routing table of each router in the path contains the address of only the next hop in the path of packet. This method is suitable for short distances only.

questions.question: What is the correct order of the operations of OSPF? i " Hello packets ii " Propagation of link-state information and building of routing tables iii " Establishing adjacencies and synchronization of database
questions.explanation: OSPF first implements a hello protocol. Then it later on tries to establish synchronisation with database. Later on building of routing tables is done.

questions.question: Which of the following is an advantage of RMON?
options: It reduces the necessity of agents in the network to be visible at all times to the NMS
It provides additional support for vendor specific MIB modules
It is an excellent medium for fault management
It is widely accepted and works good for device monitoring
questions.explanation: The use of RMON devices has several advantages. The overall benefits of implementing RMON technology in a network are higher network availability for users and greater productivity for administrators. One of the advantages of RMON is that it reduces the necessity of agents in the network to be visible at all times to the NMS. Other options convey the advantages of SNMP that are different from those of RMON.

questions.question: Which address is obtained in return if a router sends an ARP query packet?
questions.explanation: An ARP query packet contains both the physical address and logical address of the sender(router) and logical address of the receiver. This packet is used to acquire the physical address of the destination host. This physical address of the receiver helps the datagram to pass through physical network.

questions.question: Which operating mode of telnet is full duplex?
questions.explanation: In line mode, terminal character processing is done on the client side but editing is enabled on the server side. Line mode reduces the number of packets and is useful for long delay networks.

questions.question: Which of the following demerits does Fragmentation have?
questions.explanation: Fragmentation makes the implementation of the IP protocol complex and can also be exploited by attackers to create a DOS attack such as a teardrop attack. Fragmentation won"t be required if the transport layer protocols perform wise segmentation.

questions.question: What is the function of an XML interpreter in SNMPv3 Architecture?
options: It converts the XML PDU to SNMP PDU
It converts SNMP PDU to XML
It encrypts the PDU converted to XML
It deciphers the PDU encrypted in XML
questions.explanation: An SNMP security management architecture that is policy-based is composed of several components. XML interpreter converts the XML PDU to SNMP PDU. It then transfers these SNMP PDUs to other applications.

questions.question: What is the disadvantage of the burst errors over single bit errors in error detection and correction?
questions.explanation: The burst errors can be detected easily but it is difficult to correct them, as they happen to appear as blocks. When the error rate is low, burst errors are convenient to detect compared to single bit errors.

questions.question: Which of the following is the multiple access protocol for channel access control?
questions.explanation: In CSMA/CD, it deals with detection of collision after collision has occurred, whereas CSMA/CA deals with preventing collision. CSMA/CD is abbreviation for Carrier Sensing Multiple Access/Collision detection. CSMA/CA is abbreviation for Carrier Sensing Multiple Access/Collision Avoidance. These protocols are used for efficient multiple channel access.

questions.question: How can we obtain IP address using nslookup?
options: By repeatedly executing ping with a large repeat count
By calculating the difference in round-trip times for different packet sizes from each end of the link
By sending the query to the local domain name server
By finding all the hosts on a local area network (LAN) using a broadcast ping on the LAN
questions.explanation: Dig or nslookup can help when one wants to find all the hosts on a local area network (LAN). This can be done using a broadcast ping on the LAN. We need to know the IP address to execute this command, which we may not know. However, if we know a host name on the LAN, we could obtain the IP address using nslookup.

questions.question: Find the oddly matched HTTP status codes
questions.explanation: 404 Not Found.

questions.question: The local operating system on the server machine passes the incoming packets to the ___________
questions.explanation: The local operating system on the server machine passes the incoming packets to the server stub which then processes the packets which contain the queries from the client machines for retrieving information.

questions.question: WAN stands for __________
questions.explanation: WAN is the abbreviation for Wide Area Network. This network extends over a large geographical area. These are used to connect cities, states or even countries. They can be connected through leased lines or satellites.

questions.question: When a DNS server accepts and uses incorrect information from a host that has no authority giving that information, then it is called _________
questions.explanation: In DNS spoofing, also known as DNS cache poisoning, an attacker gets the valid credentials from a victim by spoofing the intended resource, and tricking the victim to give his/her valid authorization credentials.

questions.question: The Identifier that is used for data transfer in virtual circuit network is called _______
questions.explanation: A virtual circuit identifier is a type of numeric identifying address that is used to distinguish between different virtual circuits in a circuit-switched network. It is used for data transfer and has a switch scope.

questions.question: Although protocol analyzer could be used for the analysis of any line, it is primarily used in the _______ environment.
questions.explanation: The protocol analyzer is a powerful and versatile network management tool. Although it could be used for the analysis of any line, its primary use is in the LAN environment. It consists of a data capture device that is attached to a LAN.

questions.question: Which NetWare protocol provides link-state routing?
questions.explanation: NetWare Link Services Protocol (NLSP) provides link-state routing. SAP (Service Advertisement Protocol) advertises network services. NCP (NetWare Core Protocol) provides client-to-server connections and applications. RIP is a distance vector routing protocol. NLSP was developed by Novell to replace RIP routing protocols.

questions.question: In OSI model, when data is sent from device A to device B, the 5th layer to receive data at B is _________
questions.explanation: In OSI reference model, the fifth layer is Session layer. Session layer provides the mechanism for opening, closing and managing a session between end-user application processes. In TCP/IP protocol stack, the functions of the session layer are handled by the transport layer itself and thus the session layer is missing from the TCP/IP model.

questions.question: As a system administrator, you want to debug IGRP but are worried that the 'debug IP IGRP transaction' command will flood the console. What is the command that you should use?
questions.explanation: The "debug IP IGRP events" is used to display a short summary of IGRP routing information. You can append an IP address onto either console's command-line to see only the IGRP updates from a neighbor. The command will only give a short summary and hence won"t flood the command line.

questions.question: What are nodes in a computer network?
options: the computer that routes the data
the computer that terminates the data
the computer that originates the data
questions.explanation: In a computer network, a node can be anything that is capable of sending data or receiving data or even routing the data to its destination. Routers, Computers and Smartphones are some examples of network nodes.

questions.question: Communication between a computer and a keyboard involves ______________ transmission.
questions.explanation: In simplex transmission, data flows in single direction which in this case refers to the data flowing from the keyboard to the computer. Another example would be of the mouse where the data flows from the mouse to the computer only.

questions.question: _______ is an application oriented traffic that can be analyzed using performance-monitoring tools.
questions.explanation: Performance-monitoring tools can gather statistics of all protocol layers. We can analyze the application-oriented traffic such as Web-traffic. The statistics on applications could be used to make policy decisions on managing the applications.

questions.question: When a collection of various computers appears as a single coherent system to its clients, what is this called?
questions.explanation: A Computer network is defined as a collection of interconnected computers which uses a single technology for connection. A distributed system is also the same as computer network but the main difference is that the whole collection of computers appears to its users as a single coherent system. Example:- World wide web

questions.question: A web cookie is a small piece of data that is _______
options: sent from a website and stored in user's web browser while a user is browsing a website
sent from user and stored in the server while a user is browsing a website
sent from root server to all servers
sent from the root server to other root servers
questions.explanation: A web cookie is a small piece of data sent from a website and stored in user's web browser while a user is browsing the website and is used to remember stateful information about the user's operations on the website. This can help the website provide a better browsing experience to the user.

questions.question: Which of the following modulation schemes is supported by WiMAX?
questions.explanation: WiMAX supports a variety of modulation schemes such as binary phase shift keying modulation, quadrature phase shift keying modulation, and quadrature amplitude modulation and allows for the scheme to change on a burst-by-burst basis per link, depending on channel conditions.

questions.question: The data field cannot carry which of the following?
questions.explanation: Data field usually has transport layer segments, but it can also carry ICMP messages. SMTP is an application layer protocol. First it must go through the transport layer to be converted into TCP segments and then it can be inserted into IP packets.

questions.question: ISP exchanges internet traffic between their networks by __________
questions.explanation: ISPs exchange internet traffic between their networks by using Internet Exchange Points. ISPs and CDNs are connected to each other at these physical locations are they help them provide better service to their customers.

questions.question: _________ is a collective term for a number of Ethernet Standards that carry traffic at the nominal rate of 1000 Mbit/s against the original Ethernet speed of 10 Mbit/s.
questions.explanation: Fast Ethernet is a set of Ethernet Standards which were introduced in 1995, that carry traffic at the nominal rate of 1000 Mbit/s. 100BASE-TX is the most commonly used Fast Ethernet standard.

questions.question: For default gateway, which of following commands will you use on a Cisco router?
questions.explanation: IP default network command is used to find the default gateway in Cisco router. If the router finds routes to the node, it considers the routes to that node for installation as the gateway to it.

questions.question: SCP protocol is evolved from __________ over SSH.
questions.explanation: RCP is the abbreviation for Rate Control Protocol is a congestion control algorithm for fast user response times.

questions.question: EIGRP uses the ____________ algorithm for finding shortest path.
questions.explanation: EIGRP uses the DUAL algorithm for finding shortest path. DUAL stands for diffusing update algorithm and it is used to prevent routing loops by recalculating routes globally.

questions.question: Beyond IP, UDP provides additional services such as _______
questions.explanation: De-multiplexing is the delivering of received segments to the correct application layer processes at the recipients end using UDP. Error checking is done through checksum in UDP.

questions.question: Application layer is implemented in ____________
questions.explanation: Not only application layer, but presentation layer, session layer and transport layer are also implemented in the end system. The layers below are implemented outside the end system, for example, the network layer is implemented on the routers and the physical layer is implemented for the medium.

questions.question: Which of the following is an advantage of symmetric cipher?
options: (N(N-1))/2 keys are required for N people
sharing secret key over communication channel
Brute force attack for small key size
questions.explanation: Quick encryption and decryption is done in symmetric key cryptography. Because, it uses less resources and cipher text length will be small or same as plain text. It encrypts huge amount of data quickly and provides confidentiality.

questions.question: Which technique can be used in multicast routing to overcome the problem of count-to-infinity which occurs in distance vector routing?
questions.explanation: Basically, flooding in distance vector routing causes the loops in multicast routing. In order to overcome this problem, reverse path forwarding does not allow the router to send the packets to the link that on which it has received the packet.

questions.question: Collisions are improbable in _________ networks.
questions.explanation: Token passing is a channel access method used in Local Area Networks (LANs). It helps in preventing collision in the network. This method uses Ring topology.

questions.question: Which of the following explains Cookies nature?
questions.explanation: Cookies are transient by default; the values they store last for the duration of the web browser session but are lost when the user exits the browser. While the browsing session is active the cookie stores the user values in the user's storage itself and accesses them.

questions.question: You have an interface on a router with the IP address of 192.168.192.10/29. Including the router interface, how many hosts can have IP addresses on the LAN attached to the router interface?
questions.explanation: A /29 (255.255.255.248), regardless of the class of address, has only 3 host bits. Six hosts are the maximum number of hosts on this LAN, including the router interface. Out of the 8 addresses possible with the host bits, the first and the last address are for the subnet id and broadcast address respectively.

questions.question: In SONET, STS-1 level of electrical signalling has the data rate of _________
questions.explanation: STS-1 level provides the data rate of 51.84 Mbps, STS-3 provides a data rate of 155.52 Mbps, STS-12 provides a data rate of 622.080 Mbps and STS-48 provides a data rate of 2488.320 Mbps.

questions.question: The TTL field has value 10. How many routers (max) can process this datagram?
questions.explanation: TTL stands for Time to Live. This field specifies the life of the IP packet based on the number of hops it makes (Number of routers it goes through). TTL field is decremented by one each time the datagram is processed by a router. When the value is 0, the packet is automatically destroyed.

questions.question: To achieve reliable transport in TCP, ___________ is used to check the safe and sound arrival of data.
questions.explanation: Acknowledgment mechanism is used to check the safe and sound arrival of data. The sender actively checks for acknowledgement from the receiver and once a specific time period has passed, it retransmits the data.

questions.question: In a simple echo-request message, the value of the sum is 01010000 01011100. Then, value of checksum is ___________
questions.explanation: The sender side adds the bits of the fragmented packet to find a sum. Checksum is the compliment of the sum (exchange 0's and 1's). The receiver then has to verify the checksum by adding the bits of the received packet to ensure that the packet is error-free.

questions.question: The conditional GET mechanism
options: Imposes conditions on the objects to be requested
Limits the number of response from a server
Helps to keep a cache upto date
questions.explanation: The HTTP protocol requests the server of the website its trying to access so that it can store its files, images etc. in cache memory. This request of asking the server for a document considering a specific parameter is called conditional GET Request.

questions.question: Which command is used to change the interface status of a port or VLAN?
questions.explanation: "no shutdown" command is used to change the interface status of a port or VLAN to active or "UP". "no auto-summary" is a command used to prevent classful summary of IP addresses, "interface fastEthernet 0/1" is used to access the interface for ethernet port 0/1 and "copy run start" is used to copy the running configuration over the start-up configuration.

questions.question: One of the most obvious places to put an IDS sensor is near the firewall. Where exactly in relation to the firewall is the most productive placement?
options: Both inside and outside the firewall
Neither inside the firewall nor outside the firewall.
questions.explanation: There are legitimate political, budgetary and research reasons to want to see all the 'attacks' against your connection, but given the care and feeding any IDS requires, do yourself a favor and keep your NIDS sensors on the inside of the firewall.

questions.question: RPF stands for __________
questions.explanation: Reverse Path Forwarding is a loop-free forwarding method for multi-cast routing in modern systems. The method focuses on forwarding the packet away from the source IP in each iteration to make sure there is no loops.

questions.question: In OSPF, which protocol is used to discover neighbour routers automatically?
questions.explanation: Hello protocol is used to discover neighboring routers automatically. It makes sure that the communication between neighbors is bidirectional. It's similar to the real world moral construct of saying "Hello" to initialize the communication.

questions.question: Frame Relay networks offer an option called _______
questions.explanation: Frame Relay networks offer an option called Voice over Frame Relay, which transmits voice and voice-band data over a Frame Relay network. It has two sub-protocols FRF11 and FRF12.

questions.question: The ______ field is used to detect errors over the entire user datagram.
questions.explanation: Checksum field is used to detect errors over the entire user datagram. Though it is not as efficient as CRC which is used in TCP, it gets the job done for the UDP datagram as UDP doesn"t have to ensure the delivery of the packet.

questions.question: You have a network that needs 29 subnets while maximizing the number of host addresses available on each subnet. How many bits must you borrow from the host field to provide the correct subnet mask?
questions.explanation: A 240 mask is 4 subnet bits and provides 16 subnets, each with 14 hosts. We need more subnets, so let's add subnet bits. One more subnet bit would be a 248 mask. This provides 5 subnet bits (32 subnets) with 3 host bits (6 hosts per subnet). This is the best answer.

questions.question: ____________ is considered as a network node and also network security.
questions.explanation: Firewall is an entity that is a network security component but it is also considered as a network node. Malware and Ransomware are generally cyber security threats and do not qualify under network security components. Mobile secure gateway on the other is a defense method against cyber threats and is not a network node.

questions.question: Which command is used to configure telnet for remote access?
questions.explanation: "line vty 0 4" is used to configure telnet for remote access, "0 4" specifies the number of simultaneous users in this case 5. "login" command is used to specify when the password is to be asked, "enable" is used to access privileged mode and "interface fastEthernet 0/1" is used to access the interface for ethernet port 0/1.

questions.question: What is internet?
options: A network of interconnected local area networks
questions.explanation: The internet is a global network formed by connecting wide area networks (WANs), enabling worldwide communication and data sharing.

questions.question: Which of the following controlled access protocol is known as master slave method?
questions.explanation: Polling is the method for channel allocation where one station acts as a server and remaining stations acts according to the decision of master station. This master station also known as primary device, which uses two functions called poll function and select function to send and receive data within the network.

questions.question: What is the use of a trunk system in the Operations Support System for Network Transmission?
options: It is used for planning and implementation of adequate facilities for traffic to be handled without being blocked
It is used to change operations system, as it is also used to control the network and network elements
It is used to monitor the loss and signal-to-noise ratio in the trunk transmission system in Bell System
It is used to make network management system interoperable
questions.explanation: The trunk test system is used to monitor the loss and signal-to-noise ratio in the trunk transmission system in Bell System. A trunk is a logical entity linking two switching offices.

questions.question: SMTP uses which of the following TCP port?
questions.explanation: Port 21 is used for FTP control connection, port 22 is used by SSH, and port 23 is used by TELNET.

questions.question: In windows ____________ can be used to trace the route of the packet from the source to the destination.
questions.explanation: Tracert is used in case of windows, whereas Traceroute in UNIX. Tracert is a program used to find the shortest route to the destination IP. The Router sends destination unreachable message if a path to the destination IP is not found.

questions.question: The number of layers in ISO OSI reference model is __________
questions.explanation: In OSI reference model, there are 7 layers namely Application, Presentation, Session, Transport, Network, Data Link and Physical layer. Each layer uses a protocol to perform its designated function, for example, the data link layer uses error detection protocols for error control functions.

questions.question: What is the function of SMI?
options: It deals with the storage of management information
It addresses operation and administration of functional model
It describes how the management information is structured
It provides a complete model for network management
questions.explanation: The information model of the OSI network management deals with Structured Management Information (SMI) and information database known as Management Information Base (MIB). Here the SMI helps in defining the structure of management information.

questions.question: Which of the following HTTP methods should be idempotent in nature?
questions.explanation: DELETE operation should be idempotent, means their result will always same no matter how many times these operations are invoked. Also, the PUT operation is supposed to be idempotent.

questions.question: Which of these is not applicable for IP protocol?
questions.explanation: IP does not provide reliable delivery service for the data. It's dependent upon the transport layer protocols like TCP to offer reliability.

questions.question: Ethernet protocol is specified by __________
questions.explanation: IEEE 802.3 standard is for bus networks. IEEE 802.3 standard defines the MAC layer and Physical layer of the Ethernet. Hence it forms the foundation for specification of Ethernet protocol. IEEE 802.2 standard is used for specifying logical link control above the Data Link Layer of the OSI model. IEEE 802.5 standard is used for Token-ring topology. IEEE 802.11 specifies the set of protocols for implementing Wireless LAN (WLAN).

questions.question: The ATM RMON MIB is under the experimental node of the _____ lnternet MIB.
questions.explanation: There are four different collection perspectives that are possible for ATM RMON. The ATM RMON MIB is under the experimental node of the IETF lnternet MIB.

questions.question: Which of the following function is used as a consequence of not including semicolons, Commas or whitespace in the Cookie value?
questions.explanation: Cookie values cannot include semicolons, commas, or whitespace. For this reason, you may want to use the core JavaScript global function encodeURIComponent() to encode the value before storing it in the cookie.

questions.question: When discussing IDS/IPS, what is a signature?
options: It refers to 'normal,' baseline network behavior
It is used to authorize the users on a network
An electronic signature used to authenticate the identity of a user on the network
questions.explanation: IDSes work in a manner similar to modern antivirus technology. They are constantly updated with attack-definition files (signatures) that describe each type of known malicious activity. Nemean is a popular signature generation method for conventional computer networks.

questions.question: Which of the following is not a property of public key cryptography?
options: Every user has a unique private key
Public key can be same for number of users
Cannot deduce private key from public key
questions.explanation: There will be a unique public key for every user, who publishes it. Public key prevents spoofing, by acting as the receiver. Sender uses the public key of the receiver to encrypt the message and the receiver will decrypt the message with his secret key.

questions.question: What is document object model (DOM)?
options: convention for representing and interacting with objects in html documents
questions.explanation: DOM is a hierarchical model i.e. a tree used to represent an HTML or XML document. Every node of the tree an object that represents a part of the document.

questions.question: Which of the following is incorrect about hubs?
questions.explanation: A switch has no intelligence, and it does not need information about the routes as it uses broadcast messages. Regardless of routes are routing tables and broadcast messages will be sent to each port.

questions.question: Teredo is an automatic tunneling technique. In each client the obfuscated IPv4 address is represented by bits ______
questions.explanation: Teredo is a technique through which gives the possibility for full IPv6 network connectivity to IPv6 capable hosts which are currently on an IPv4 network. Bits 96 to 127 in the datagram represents obfuscated 1Pv4 address of the IPv4 network.

questions.question: The technique in which a congested node stops receiving data from the immediate upstream node or nodes is called as _______
questions.explanation: In this closed loop congestion control technique, the congested node propagates in the opposite direction of the data flow to inform the predecessor node to reduce the flow of packets. This is why this technique is called a node-to-node congestion control technique.

questions.question: Who are three discoverers of the RSA algorithm in public key cryptography?
questions.explanation: RSA algorithm is a public key algorithm, which was discovered at M.I.T in 1978. It is very strong but the only disadvantage is, it requires more key length in order to provide more security.

questions.question: In asymmetric key cryptography, the private key is kept by __________
options: all the connected devices to the network
questions.explanation: The private key is kept only by the receiver of the message. Its aim is to make sure that only the intended receiver can decipher the message.

questions.question: What is the term for an endpoint of an inter-process communication flow across a computer network?
questions.explanation: Socket is one end point in a two way communication link in the network. TCP layer can identify the application that data is destined to be sent by using the port number that is bound to socket.

questions.question: _________ is an Upper Layer Protocol that provides Flow Control.
questions.explanation: SMTP is an upper level protocol and it provides flow control. Whereas Frame relay Protocol operates in the layer such as Data Link Layer that is at lower levels of OSI model. Thus it doesn"t provide flow control or error control. Similarly other protocols such as TCP and ICMP operate in the lower levels of the OSI model.

questions.question: The first Network was called ________
questions.explanation: ARPANET stands for Advanced Research Projects Agency Networks. It was the first network to be implemented which used the TCP/IP protocol in the year 1969.

questions.question: Which connector does the STP cable use?
questions.explanation: RJ-45 is used for STP cable. 100 Mbps is the max data transfer rate that can be handled by STP. RJ-45 is popularly used to connect to modern-day routers, computer network cards, and other network devices.

questions.question: There are four logical layers of TMN wherein the Service Management Layer ____________
options: analyzes business aspects, trends and billing options
performs configuration, control, and distributes network resources
handles network elements, network information and helps in maintenance
handles administration, information and charges of service in a network
questions.explanation: TMN has four logical layers that are based on the network management services: business, network, service, and element. These layers are Business Management, Network Management, Service Management, and Element Management. Thus, Service Management Layer looks after services in a network and its administration.

questions.question: In Fault management system, what can be used to track network problems?
questions.explanation: Fault management involves the detection and isolation of the problem causing the failure in the network. A trouble ticket is generated and followed up for resolution of the problem using a trouble ticket administration system. Thus, a trouble ticket administration helps in tracking network problems.

questions.question: ___________ is necessary when the sender wants to use IPv6, but the receiver does not understand IPv6.
questions.explanation: Header translation is used when the sender wants to use IPv6, but the receiver does not understand IPv6. It is made possible through a Network Address Translation " Protocol Translation enabled device such as a gateway.

questions.question: The IEEE standards for Local Area Network (LAN) and Metropolitan Area Network (MAN) specifications are concerned with OSI layers __________ and _________
questions.explanation: There are a set of IEEE standards that are used for network management. IEEE 802 standards are defined in Physical and Data Link Layers of OSI Model. It defines protocols, services etc. for Local Area Network (LAN), Metropolitan Area Network (MAN) and other Wireless Networks.

questions.question: Which one of the following is not an application layer protocol used in internet?
questions.explanation: Resource reservation protocol is a transport layer protocol used on the internet. It operates over IPv4 and IPv6 and is designed to reserve resources required by the network layer protocols.

questions.question: SSH can be used in only _____________
questions.explanation: SSH isn't confined to a certain network or operating system. It can be implemented over different networks and on different operating systems.

questions.question: Datagrams are routed to their destinations with the help of ________
questions.explanation: Routing table is used to route the packets to their destinations. The packet/datagram header contains the destination header for the whole journey to source to the destination through the routers.

questions.question: ASN.1 is a formal language developed jointly by _______ and _______ for use with application layers for data transfer between systems.
questions.explanation: ASN.1 is a standard language used for describing messages sent and received in a network. It is a joint standard developed by International Telecommunication Union (ITU-T) and International Organization for Standardization (ISO).

questions.question: Which one of the following is not used to generate dynamic web pages?
questions.explanation: CSS alone cannot be used to generate dynamic web pages as it does not provide many event handling functions. It can be used along with JavaScript to generate dynamic web pages which are visually compelling.

questions.question: What is Ci in the bit-by-bit XOR hash function, that is Ci = bi1 + bi2...... + bim
options: ith bit of the hash code
questions.explanation: "m" is the number of n-bit blocks in the input, bij is the ith bit in the jth block, ci is the ith bit of the hash code. Operation between the bits is exclusive OR and this operation is performed on every input block.

questions.question: Which of the following is an advantage of the link state routing?
questions.explanation: Link state routing causes less loops when compared to distance vector routing. It is because, complete topology information will be there at each router and this information prevents misunderstandings which leads to loops.

questions.question: What will happen if two networks choose the policy called peering in place of transit service that is given by a provider?
questions.explanation: The traffic that autonomous systems get when they do peering is low compared to the traffic that they get when they use provider. And also, the provider lets its other customers to know the path of these ASs and it leads to more traffic to these ASs.

questions.question: What is the function of Masquerade in SNMPv3 security threats?
options: Some unauthorized user may modify the contents of the message while it is in transit
The message stream could be monitored by an unauthorized person by eavesdropping
An unauthorized user sends information to another assuming the identity of an authorized user
An authorized user is denied service by a management entity
questions.explanation: Masquerade is when an unauthorized user sends information to another assuming the identity of an authorized user. This can be done by changing the originating address. Using the masquerade and modification of information, an unauthorized user can perform an operation on a management entity, which he or she is not permitted to do. The SNMP set operation should be protected against this attack.

questions.question: Which protocol provides the synchronization between media streams?
questions.explanation: RTCP stands for Real-time Transport Control Protocol and it works with RTP to send control packets to the users of the networks while RTP handles the actual data delivery.

questions.question: Pick the odd one out.
questions.explanation: File transfer, File download and Email are services provided by the application layer and there are message and data oriented.

questions.question: _________ is another kind of fiber optic network with an active star for switching.
questions.explanation: A 50-MBd active star fiber optical Local area network (LAN) and its optical combiner and mixing rod splitter are presented. The limited power budget and relatively large tapping losses of light wave technology, which limit the use of fiber optics in tapped bus LAN topologies, are examined and proven tolerable in optical star topologies.

questions.question: What is start frame delimeter (SFD) in ethernet frame?
questions.explanation: The start frame delimiter is a 1 byte field in the Ethernet frame that indicates that the preceding bits are the start of the frame. It is always set to 10101011.

questions.question: The DoS attack, in which the attacker establishes a large number of half-open or fully open TCP connections at the target host is ________
questions.explanation: In Vulnerability attack, the attacker exploits the vulnerable control points of the network to deny access to the victims. In Bandwidth flooding, the attacker intentionally uses up all the bandwidth by flooding the victim with a deluge of packets and makes sure that the victim can"t use any bandwidth. In UDP flooding, too many UDP packets are sent by the attacker to the victim at random ports.

questions.question: The DHCP server _________
options: maintains a database of available IP addresses
maintains the information about client configuration parameters
grants a IP address when receives a request from a client
questions.explanation: Whenever a DHCP server gets a request from a client it responds with a DHCP offer containing IP address being offered, network mask offered, the amount of time that the client can use and keep it, the ip address of the DHCP server making this offer.

questions.question: What are the two broad categories of symmetric key cryptography?
questions.explanation: Symmetric key cryptography has two types; classical and modern. Classical cryptography again divided into two types; transposition and substitution. Modern cryptography is of two types; stream cipher and block cipher.

questions.question: Which transport layer protocols are reliable?
questions.explanation: TCP and SCTP are connection oriented protocols and they ensure that there is no packet loss. Also, these protocols use different methods and mechanisms such as flow control, error control, congestion control and acknowledgments to achieve reliability.

questions.question: What is the normal virtual area network range?
questions.explanation: VLAN 2-1001 is a normal range where we can create the VLANs. These VLANs are editable means we can create, delete and update. Actually 1-4094 is the complete valid range to create the VLANs.

questions.question: At which interval does OSPF refresh LSAs?
questions.explanation: Each LSA has its own age timer. By default, an LSA waits for 30 minutes before requiring an update. So to make sure that each router first has an up-to-date LSA, OSPF refreshes LSAs after every 1 hour.

questions.question: Which one of the following is a file transfer protocol using SSH?
questions.explanation: SCP (Secure copy protocol), SFTP (SSH File Transfer Protocol) and Rsync all are file transfer protocols which are used by SSH.

questions.question: The networks that needs central monitoring, still uses __________
questions.explanation: There are three significant types of Terminals used by LAN. Those are smart terminal, intelligent terminal and dumb terminal. The dumb terminal does not carry out the function of data processing. In central monitoring systems we need terminals that will just carry out the tasks of sending and receiving data packets. Thus dumb terminal is used in such networks.

questions.question: All telnet operations are sent as ________
questions.explanation: Telnet provides a bi-directional, 8-bit byte oriented communications facility through which operations are sent as 8-bit bytes for the server to interpret.

questions.question: Which of the following field in IPv4 datagram is not related to fragmentation?
questions.explanation: TOS-type of service identifies the type of packets. It is not related to fragmentation but is used to request specific treatment such as high throughput, high reliability or low latency for the IP packet depending upon the type of service it belongs to.

questions.question: Which protocol is a signaling communication protocol used for controlling multimedia communication sessions?
questions.explanation: SIP is a signaling protocol in which its function includes initiating, maintaining and terminating real time sessions. SIP is used for signaling and controlling multimedia sessions.

questions.question: The PPP protocol _________
options: Is designed for simple links which transport packets between two peers
Is one of the protocols for making an Internet connection over a phone line
Is designed for simple links which transport packets between two peers and making an Internet connection over a phone line
questions.explanation: The PPP protocol is designed for handling simple links which transport packets between two peers. It is a standard protocol that is used to make an Internet connection over phone lines.

questions.question: Which field helps to check rearrangement of the fragments?
questions.explanation: The Fragment Offset field specifies where the fragment fits in the original datagram. The offset of the first fragment will always be 0. The size of the field (13 bits) is 3-bits shorter than the size of the total length field (16 bits).

questions.question: Dual-stack approach refers to _________
options: node has both IPv4 and IPv6 support
implementing a MAC address with 2 stacks
questions.explanation: Dual-stack is one of the approaches used to support IPv6 in already existing systems. ISPs are using it as a method to transfer from IPv4 to IPv6 completely eventually due to the lower number of possible available addresses in IPv4.

questions.question: In open-loop control, policies are applied to __________
questions.explanation: Open loop congestion control techniques are used to prevent congestion before it even happens by enforcing certain policies. Retransmission policy, window policy and acknowledgement policy are some policies that might be enforced.

questions.question: Firewalls are often configured to block ___________
questions.explanation: UDP is more vulnerable to attacks, so firewalls are often configured to block suspicious UDP traffic.

questions.question: Find the FTP reply whose message is wrongly matched.
questions.explanation: The correct response code for the message "Can"t open data connection" is 425. Response code 452 is sent usually when the connection is suddenly closed.

questions.question: In a Frame Relay network ____________ is a device that converts data packets into frames.
questions.explanation: Frame Relay Access Device or Frame Delay Assembler/Dissembler (FRAD) is a device that converts data packets into frames that can be sent over Frame Relay network. It also converts frames from Frame Relay into data packets. Whereas FRWAN stands for Frame relay WAN, VoFR stands for Voice over Frame Relay and FRS stands for Frame Relay Switch. Each of these entities carries out tasks that are different from FRAD.

questions.question: The values GET, POST, HEAD etc are specified in ____________ of HTTP message
questions.explanation: It is specified in the method field of request line in the HTTP request message.

questions.question: An RTP header has a minimum size of _________
questions.explanation: Each RTP packet has a fixed header of size 12 bytes that contains essential control information like timestamp, payload type etc. for the receiving system processing.

questions.question: Suppose a TCP connection is transferring a file of 1000 bytes. The first byte is numbered 10001. What is the sequence number of the segment if all data is sent in only one segment?
questions.explanation: The sequence number given to first byte of a segment, with respect to its order among the previous segments, is the sequence number of that segment.

questions.question: Frame Relay does not provide flow or error control, they must be provided by the ______
questions.explanation: Frame relay only provides error detection using CRC. If errors are detected, the upper-layer protocols, such as TCP are expected to provide error correction features. Network layer provides flow control.

questions.question: Control of the users" access to network resources through charges is the main responsibility of ________
questions.explanation: The accounting management keeps track of the users and their access rights to the network and controls the user's access by communicating with the security management. The accounting management takes support of the Management Information Block to perform its operations.

questions.question: What is the max length of the Shielded twisted pair cable?
questions.explanation: The max the Shielded twisted pair cable is 100 meters. If the length exceeds 100 meters, the loss of signals flowing through the cable would be really high. Thus, STP cable is more suitable for smaller networks like LANs.

questions.question: With respect to physical media, STP cables stands for _________
questions.explanation: For physical media, STP cable stands for Shielded twisted pair cable. 100 Mbps is the max data capacity of STP cable and its default connector is RJ45. It is popularly used in LANs due to its ease of maintenance and installation.

questions.question: A second technique to reduce routing table and simplify searching process is called _________
questions.explanation: In the network specific forwarding method, there is only one record, the destination of the packet, in the routing table and not the other hosts of the network. The other two forwarding methods are the default method and the next-hop method.

questions.question: DNS database contains _______
questions.explanation: Domain Name system not only deals with mapping IP addresses with the hostname but also deals with exchange of information in the server.

questions.question: You have an IP address of 172.16.13.5 with a 255.255.255.128 subnet mask. What is your class of address, subnet address, and broadcast address?
questions.explanation: We know that the prefix 172 lies in class B (128 to 191) of IPv4 addresses. From the subnet mask, we get that the class is divided into 2 subnets: 172.16.13.0 to 172.16.13.127 and 172.16.13.128 to 172.16.13.255. The IP 172.16.13.5 lies in the first subnet. So the starting address 172.16.13.0 is the subnet address and last address 172.16.13.127 is the broadcast address.

questions.question: What is the cipher text for the message "TAKE ME TO THE PARTY", id Rail Fence technique is used with two rows?
questions.explanation: Rail fence is the transposition technique, that belongs to classical cryptography. First the plain text is written in a sequence of diagonals with defined number of rows, then the cipher text is read off as a sequence of all the rows from starting to end.

questions.question: Bluetooth uses __________
questions.explanation: Frequency hopping spread spectrum is a method of transmitting radio signals by rapidly changing the carrier frequency and is controlled by the codes known to the sender and receiver only.

questions.question: To determine which version to use when sending a packet to a destination, the source host queries which of the following?
questions.explanation: Source host queries DNS to determine which version to use when sending a packet to a destination. The DNS contains both, the IPv4 and IPv6 addresses of the modern dual stack host servers.

questions.question: Application layer offers _______ service.
options: Both End to end and Process to process
questions.explanation: End to End service is provided in the application layer. Whereas process to process service is provided at the transport layer.

questions.question: Security management is both a/an _______ and a/an _______ issue in information management.
questions.explanation: Security management is both a technical and administrative issue in information management. It involves securing access to the network and information flowing in the network, access to data stored in the network, and manipulating the data that are stored and flowing across the network.

questions.question: Which of the following protocol is used by Restful web services as a medium of communication between client and server?
