questions.explanation: Restful web services make use of HTTP protocol as a medium of communication between client and server. The REST architecture was known as the HTTP object model back in the year 1994.

questions.question: How to create a VLAN in a switch?
questions.explanation: "vlan" is the command used to create a VLAN. "name HR" is used to assign a name to a VLAN, "interface vlan" is used to access the interface of a VLAN and "no vlan" command is used to delete a VLAN.

questions.question: _______ standard is used by Wireless LAN.
questions.explanation: IEEE 802.11 specifies the set of protocols for implementing Wireless LAN (WLAN). IEEE 802.3 standard is for bus networks. IEEE 802.3 standard defines the MAC layer and Physical layer of the Ethernet. Hence it forms the foundation for the specification of Ethernet protocol. IEEE 802.2 standard is used for specifying logical link control above the Data Link Layer of the OSI model. IEEE 802.5 standard is used for Token-ring topology.

questions.question: In the IPv6 header, the traffic class field is similar to which field in the IPv4 header?
questions.explanation: The traffic class field is used to specify the priority of the IP packet which is a similar functionality to the Type of Service field in the IPv4 header. It's an 8-bit field and its values are not defined in the RFC 2460.

questions.question: A 4 byte IP address consists of __________
questions.explanation: An ip address which is 32 bits long, that means it is of 4 bytes and is composed of a network and host portion and it depends on address class. The size of the host address and network address depends upon the class of the address in classful IP addressing.

questions.question: Which of the following is false with respect to the features of OSPF?
options: Support for fixed-length subnetting by including the subnet mask in the routing message
More flexible link cost than can range from 1 to 65535
Distribution of traffic over multiple paths that have equal cost to the destination
questions.explanation: OSPF provides support for variable-length sunbathing by including the subnet mask in the routing message. For fixed length subnets, there is no requirement for including the subnet mask in the routing message as there is just one subnet mask for all the subnets.

questions.question: What is the full form of OSI?
questions.explanation: OSI is the abbreviation for Open System Interconnection. OSI model provides a structured plan on how applications communicate over a network, which also helps us to have a structured plan for troubleshooting. It is recognized by the ISO as the generalized model for computer network i.e. it can be modified to design any kind of computer network.

questions.question: Which of the following persistent method of random-access protocol reduces the collisions and efficiency of the network?
questions.explanation: Unlike 1- persistent in non-persistent methods, stations wait for some random time even if it finds the channel idle. This random time reduces the collision at the same time it reduces the efficiency because random times may keep the channel unused even the stations have frames to send.

questions.question: _________ speeds up the get-next-request process and is especially useful to retrieve data from tables.
questions.explanation: The Bulk Data Transfer Message has the ability to request and receive bulk data using the get-bulk message. This speeds up the get-next-request process and is especially useful to retrieve data from tables.

questions.question: The sizes of source and destination port address in TCP header are ___________ respectively.
questions.explanation: All port addresses are of 16 bits and they specify the type of service being used by the network entity. For example, port 21 is used for FTP connections and port 25 is used for ICMP connections.

questions.question: ElGamal encryption system is __________
questions.explanation: The ELGamal encryption system was made by Taher Elgamal in the year 1985 and is an asymmetric key algorithm. It is popularly used in PGP and other systems.

questions.question: Who will be the involving parties in the direct digital signature scheme?
options: Source and destination and third party
questions.explanation: In general, there will be a third party, who authenticate the digital signatures. But there is no rule that everyone should believe the third party. So, the direct digital signature scheme involves sender and receiver. And receiver knows from where the message is coming using public key of the sender.

questions.question: Suppose two IPv6 nodes want to interoperate using IPv6 datagrams, but they are connected to each other by intervening IPv4 routers. The best solution here is ________
questions.explanation: The IPv4 routers can form a tunnel in which at the sender's side, the IPv6 datagram is encapsulated in to IPv4, and at the receiver's side of the tunnel, the IPv4 packet is stripped and the IPv6 packet is sent to the receiver.

questions.question: Which of the following statements is NOT true concerning VPNs?
options: Financially rewarding compared to leased lines
Allows remote workers to access corporate data
Allows LAN-to-LAN connectivity over public networks
Is the backbone of the Internet
questions.explanation: VPNs are not the backbone of the Internet as they are just a method to create private intranets on the internet. They are used for enhancing the connection security for the users.

questions.question: Terrestrial radio channels are broadly classifed into _____ groups.
questions.explanation: The three types are those that operate over very short distance, those that operate in local areas, those that operate in the wide area.

questions.question: What is the subnet id of a host with an IP address 172.16.66.0/21?
questions.explanation: A /21 is 255.255.248.0, which means we have a block size of 8 in the third octet, so we just count by 8 until we reach 66. The subnet in this question is 64.0. The next subnet is 72.0, so the broadcast address of the 64 subnet is 71.255.

questions.question: What update can it be called, if a node sends its routing table to its neighbors if there are any modifications in the routing table?
questions.explanation: Triggered update may happen when there is change in the routing table of the node or failure of neighboring links. It can happen at any time if a node is subjected to changes.

questions.question: Geostationary satellites ___________
options: Are placed at a fixed point above the earth
Rotate the earth about a fixed axis
Rotate the earth about a varying axis
questions.explanation: They are placed in orbit at 36,000km above Earth's surface.

questions.question: The address that is unique in the scope of the network or internationally if the network is part of an international network is called as ______
questions.explanation: Global address is a network address that is unique internationally and is used as a common address by all the users of the network. It is used to create a virtual circuit identifier.

questions.question: In which routing, the destination address field contains the address of the next router?
questions.explanation: Generally, the field of destination address contains the final destination address of the packet, but in the source routing the address field is filled by the address of the router which is going to be visited next.

questions.question: TCP process may not write and read data at the same speed. So we need __________ for storage.
questions.explanation: A TCP receiver has a receive buffer that is used to store the unprocessed incoming packets in case the sender is sending packets faster than the processing rate of the received packets.

questions.question: How is a single channel shared by multiple signals in a computer network?
questions.explanation: In communication and computer networks, the main goal is to share a scarce resource. This is done by multiplexing, where multiple analog or digital signals are combined into one signal over a shared medium. The multiple kinds of signals are designated by the transport layer which is the layer present on a higher level than the physical layer.

questions.question: A client that wishes to connect to an open server tells its TCP that it needs to be connected to that particular server. The process is called ___________
questions.explanation: This is the second step in the Three-Way Handshaking process and is done by the client once it finds the open server and picks an ISN. The Server acknowledges the clients request, and then picks an ISN and synchronizes it with the Client. At last, the Client acknowledges the servers ISN.

questions.question: Identify the incorrect statement regarding FTP.
options: FTP stands for File Transfer Protocol
FTP uses two parallel TCP connections
FTP sends its control information in-band
FTP sends exactly one file over the data connection
questions.explanation: FTP is out-of-band because the data connection is done separately through port 20 and control connection is done separately through port 21.

questions.question: __________ command is used to manipulate TCP/IP routing table.
questions.explanation: The route command is used to view and manipulate the TCP/IP routing table in Windows OS. The manipulations done in the routing table with the help of this command will count as static routes.

questions.question: Which of the following statements can be associated with OSI model?
options: A structured way to discuss and easier update system components
One layer may duplicate lower layer functionality
Functionality at one layer no way requires information from another layer
It is an application specific network model
questions.explanation: The OSI model provides a standardized framework for network communication by dividing it into layers. This layered approach simplifies troubleshooting, enables modular updates, and ensures interoperability between different network devices and protocols.

questions.question: __________ allows checking if a domain is available for registration.
questions.explanation: There are billions of domains available for registration on the World Wide Web, and many of them are already registered. So when one wants to register a domain, they need to check whether the domain is available through a domain check.

questions.question: Which one of the following is an architecture paradigms?
questions.explanation: HTTP is a protocol.

questions.question: Which routing protocol implements the diffusing update algorithm?
questions.explanation: The diffusing update algorithm (DUAL) is used to maintain backup routes to a destination for when the primary route fails. EIGRP routing protocol implements the diffusing update algorithm.

questions.question: The computation of the shortest path in OSPF is usually done by ____________
questions.explanation: Shortest path in OSPF is usually computed by Dijkstra's algorithm. It was proposed by Edsger W. Dijkstra in the year 1956. It is a greedy method algorithm and hence may not guarantee the shortest path every time, but is really fast.

questions.question: Cryptanalysis is used __________
options: to find some insecurity in a cryptographic scheme
questions.explanation: Cryptanalysis is a field of study in which a cryptographic scheme is intentionally tried to breach in order to find flaws and insecurities. It is used to make sure that the scheme is least vulnerable to attacks.

questions.question: Which of the situations might not require multiple routing protocols in a network?
options: When a new Layer 2-only switch is added to the network
When you are migrating from one routing protocol to another
When you are using routers from multiple vendors
When there are host-based routers from multiple vendors
questions.explanation: Multiple routing protocols are required only when we need to migrate from one routing protocol to another, or when we are using routers from multiple vendors, or when there are host-based routers from multiple vendors. Routing is not a layer-2 function so we don"t require multiple routing protocols when new layer-2 switch is added.

questions.question: Consider a situation in which you are a system administrator on a NetWare network, you are running NetWare 4.11 and you cannot communicate with your router. What is the likely problem?
options: Cisco routers only work with NetWare 3.11
questions.explanation: The default encapsulation on Cisco routers is Novell Ethernet_802.3 and NetWare 3.12and later defaults to 802.2 encapsulation, 3.11 and earlier defaults to 802.3.

questions.question: The universal tag "ENUMERATED" in ASN.1 defines ___________
options: a string of binary digits or null set
set of values associated with the object
questions.explanation: The universal class is the most commonly used class. Data types belonging to the universal class are application-independent. ENUMERATED is an example of universal class that is used to mean a specified list of Integers.

questions.question: When 2 or more bits in a data unit has been changed during the transmission, the error is called ____________
questions.explanation: When a single bit error occurs in a data, it is called single bit error. When more than a single bit of data is corrupted or has error, it is called burst error. If a single bit error occurs, the bit can be simply repaired by inverting it, but in case of a burst error, the sender has to send the frame again.

questions.question: Why SNMP messages are exchanged using a connectionless UDP transport protocol?
options: In order to be consistent with the complexity of the SNMP model
In order to make the process slower
In order to use a heavyweight mechanism for exchanging messages
questions.explanation: SNMP messages are exchanged using a connectionless UDP transport protocol in order to reduce traffic. However the mechanisms of SNMP are suitable for a variety of protocols.

questions.question: WiMAX MAC layer provides an interface between ___________
options: higher transport layers and physical layer
data link layer and network layer
questions.explanation: WiMAX provides support for multiple physical layer (PHY) on the physical layer and Media Access Control (MAC) options for higher layers to provide wireless broadband access to buildings.

questions.question: In _________ systems, resources are allocated on demand.
questions.explanation: In packet switching, the bits are received in out of order and need to be assembled at the receiver end, whereas in the case of Circuit switching, all the bits are received in order. All transmissions may not occur through the same path in case of packet switching.

questions.question: CRC stands for __________
questions.explanation: Cyclic redundancy check is a code that is added to a data which helps us to identify any error that occurred during the transmission of the data. CRC is only able to detect errors, not correct them. CRC is inserted in the frame trailer.

questions.question: Which of the following factors affect transmission rate in DSL?
options: The gauge of the twisted-pair line
The gauge of the twisted-pair line and degree of electrical interference
questions.explanation: Because DSL is made of twisted wire copper pair, the gauge of twisted pair line i.e. the protection and electrical interference would affect the transmission rate in DSL. Unlike DSL, FTTP is not really affected by these factors.

questions.question: In wireless ad-hoc network _________
questions.explanation: An ad-hoc wireless network is a decentralized kind of a wireless network. An access point is usually a central device and it would go against the rules of the ad-hoc network to use one. Hence it is not required.

questions.question: Which one of the following feature was present in SSH protocol, version 1?
questions.explanation: All of the mentioned features are provided by SSH-2 and that SSH-1 only provide strong authentication and guarantee confidentiality.

questions.question: A _______ is the physical path over which a message travels.
questions.explanation: Messages travel from sender to receiver via a physical path called the medium using a set of methods/rules called protocol. Mediums can be guided (wired) or unguided (wireless).

questions.question: An alternative to JavaScript on windows platform is _______
questions.explanation: VBScript is a general-purpose, lightweight and active scripting language which can be used on Microsoft Visual Basic. It was first released in 1996.

questions.question: An asynchronous connection uses __________ encapsulation.
questions.explanation: In WAN technology, an asynchronous serial connection is configured using Point to Point Protocol (PPP) and Asynchronous Transfer Mode (ATM) encapsulation methods.

questions.question: Which layer of the OSI reference model does PPTP work at?
questions.explanation: PPTP stands for Point-to-Point Tunneling Protocol. PPTP is a tunneling protocol that was primitively used to create VPNs. It is no longer used for VPNs due to the lack of security it provides.

questions.question: If the value in protocol field is 17, the transport layer protocol used is _____________
questions.explanation: The protocol field enables the demultiplexing feature so that the IP protocol can be used to carry payloads of more than one protocol type. Its most used values are 17 and 6 for UDP and TCP respectively. ICMP and IGMP are network layer protocols.

questions.question: Devices that provide the connectivity to a WiMAX network are known as _________
questions.explanation: Subscriber stations in WiMAX are transceivers (transmitter and receivers). They are used to convert radio signals into digital signals that can be routed to and from communication devices. There is a variety of types of WiMAX subscriber stations like portable PCMCIA cards and fixed stations that provide service to multiple users.

questions.question: Which one of the following authentication method is used by SSH?
questions.explanation: SSH used public key authentication, Password authentication, Host based authentication, keyboard authentication and authentication of servers.

questions.question: The architecture for SNMP standard for network management is defined by _________
questions.explanation: IETF stands for Internet Engineering Task Force. It is a set of standards that implicitly defines the architecture for SNMP. SNMP network management is used for campus wide networks.

questions.question: A/An ________ message is generated by a manager application and is transmitted to another manager application.
questions.explanation: Inform-request is a message generated by manager. It is sent using a session. As mentioned above, the receiving manager application responds with a response message. This set of communication messages is a powerful enhancement in SNMPv2, since it makes network management systems interoperable.

questions.question: Virtual circuit identifier in frame relay is called _______
questions.explanation: The Data Link Connection Identifier is 10-bit virtual circuit identifier. It is used to assign frames to the specified Permanent Virtual Circuits or Switched Virtual Circuits.

questions.question: Which of the following frequencies is not used in WiMAX for communication?
questions.explanation: The 2.4GHz ISM frequency band is used for personal area network technologies such as Bluetooth and hence is not suitable for WiMAX which is mostly used for Metropolitan Area Networks.

questions.question: The technique of temporarily delaying outgoing acknowledgements so that they can be hooked onto the next outgoing data frame is called ____________
questions.explanation: Piggybacking is a technique in which the acknowledgment is temporarily delayed so as to be hooked with the next outgoing data frame. It saves a lot of channel bandwidth as in non-piggybacking system, some bandwidth is reserved for acknowledgement.

questions.question: An RPC application requires _________
options: specific protocol for client server communication
questions.explanation: The RPC technique for constructing distributed, client-server applications based on extending the conventional local procedure calling. It requires a client program, a server program and specific protocol for client server communication to build the system.

questions.question: HFC contains _______
options: A combination of Fibre cable and Coaxial cable
questions.explanation: Hybrid fiber-coaxial (HFC) is a telecommunications industry term for a broadband network that combines optical fiber and coaxial cable. It has been popularly used since the early 1990s. It is stronger than the optical fiber cables and faster than the co-axial cables.

questions.question: Identify the difficulty a SAN administrator does not incur while dealing with diverse vendors.
options: Multiple applications to manage storage in the data center
questions.explanation: A single view is not possible with diverse vendors present. Proprietary management interfaces, multiple applications management and no single view are the main difficulties incurred by a SAN administrator in such a situation.

questions.question: In EIGRP best path is known as the successor, where as backup path is known as __________
options: There is no backup route in EIGRP
questions.explanation: Feasible successor is the backup path. The backup path is used alternatively used whenever the best path fails. It is not used primarily because it is comparatively expensive than the best path.

questions.question: The state when dedicated signals are idle are called __________
questions.explanation: There are instances when connection between two endpoints has been established, but no communication or transfer of messages occurs. This period of time is called silent period. The silent period ends when either of the two endpoints starts the communication.

questions.question: POP3 protocol belongs to which layer of the ISO OSI model?
questions.explanation: POP3 protocol is an application layer protocol used by email clients to retrieve emails. Protocols like MAC, IP, and TCP, work on the Data Link layer, Network layer, and Transport layer respectively.

questions.question: Which one of the following is a cryptographic protocol used to secure HTTP connection?
questions.explanation: TLS has strong message authentication and key-material generation to prevent eavesdropping, tampering and message forgery. It has been used since the year 1996.

questions.question: Which network topology requires a central controller or hub?
questions.explanation: In star topology, no computer is connected to another computer directly but all the computers are connected to a central hub. Every message sent from a source computer goes through the hub and the hub then forwards the message only to the intended destination computer.

questions.question: Which of the following application in which hash function is employed, refers hash value as message digest?
questions.explanation: The application "message authentication" is used to obtain the integrity of the message. Sender sends hash value of the message along with message, then receiver performs the same hash function on the message. If both the message digests are same, means, integrity is achieved.

questions.question: Which protocol defines a profile of RTP that provides cryptographic services for the transfer of payload data?
questions.explanation: SRTP stands for Secure Real-time Transport Protocol. It is like an extension to RTP which provides stream security through encryption, message authentication and integrity, and replay attack protection.

questions.question: Which mail access protocol is used for accessing email from multiple clients?
questions.explanation: IMAP protocol is used when an email is to be accessed from multiple clients. POP3 is used to provide email access to a single program or client whereas SMTP is used for sending emails. SNMP is used for managing devices on the network.

questions.question: In the OSI model, as a data packet moves from the lower to the upper layers, headers are _______
questions.explanation: In OSI reference model, when data packet moves from lower layers to higher layer, headers get removed. Whereas when the data packet moves from higher layer to lower layers, headers are added. These headers contain the essential control information for the protocols used on the specific layer.

questions.question: Which error correcting code works well for burst errors among the following?
questions.explanation: Hamming codes and convolution codes works well for single bit errors, whereas Reed Solomon codes works well for burst errors. Because it uses m bit symbols, which are used to treat single bit and m bit burst errors equally as a single symbol error.

questions.question: What tells a firewall how to reassemble a data stream that has been divided into packets?
options: The number in the header's identification field
The header checksum field in the packet header
questions.explanation: The source routing feature provides a path address for the packet to help the firewall to reassemble the data stream that was divided into packets. After reassembling, the firewall can then filter the stream.

questions.question: The DoS attack, in which the attacker sends deluge of packets to the targeted host is ________
questions.explanation: In Bandwidth flooding, the attacker floods the victim machine with a deluge of packets to make sure that no bandwidth is available. The victim then cannot utilize the complete bandwidth to perform its operation.

questions.question: The characterization of the performance of a computer network is ___________ in nature.
questions.explanation: The characterization of the performance of a computer network is statistical in nature. This led to the logical step of measuring the statistics of important parameters in the network from the NOC and the development of remote monitoring (RMON) specifications.

questions.question: If 5 files are transferred from server A to client B in the same session. The number of TCP connections between A and B is _______
questions.explanation: The client would first initiate the TCP control connection through port 21. Then for every file transfer, a separate connection would be made through port 20. Now, since we have five files to be transferred, 1 control connection + 5 data connections = 6 total TCP connections.

questions.question: RPC is used to _________
options: establish a server on remote machine that can respond to queries
retrieve information by calling a query
establish a server on remote machine that can respond to queries and retrieve information by calling a query
questions.explanation: RPC or Remote Procedure Call is used to establish a server on remote machine that can respond to queries and to retrieve information by calling a query by other computers.

questions.question: Which one of the following is an SMTP server configured in such a way that anyone on the internet can send e-mail through it?
questions.explanation: Anyone can send an e-mail through an Open Mail Relay server so it acted like a free relay for email agents to forward their mails through. Open Mail Relays are now unpopular because they can be used by attackers to perform man-in-the-middle attacks.

questions.question: The photonic layer of the SONET is similar to the __________ of OSI model.
questions.explanation: The photonic layer in SONET is like the physical layer of the OSI model. It is the lowest layer among the four layers of SONET namely the photonic, the section, the line, and the path layers.

questions.question: Retransmission of packets must not be done when _______
questions.explanation: Retransmission refers to the sender having to resend the packet to the receiver. It needs to be done only when some anomaly occurs with the packet like when the packet is lost or corrupted.

questions.question: The required resources for communication between end systems are reserved for the duration of the session between end systems in ________ method.
questions.explanation: In circuit switching, a physical path between the sender and receiver is established. This path is maintained until the connection is needed. Circuit switching is implemented in the Physical layer and is used in telephonic systems.

questions.question: The transparent bridge learns routing information by a ______ learning process.
questions.explanation: A transparent bridge is also known as adaptive bridge. It uses backward learning process to gain routing information. It keeps information about all the nodes connected to specific ports.

questions.question: Which of the following defines the Cookie visibility?
questions.explanation: sessionStorage, localStorage and Document path all are used to store data on the client-side. Each one has its own storage and expiration limit. Cookie visibility is scoped by the document origin as Local Storage and Session Storage are, and also by document path.

questions.question: Which component is included in IP security?
questions.explanation: AH ensures that there is no retransmission of data from an unauthorized source, and protects against data tampering. ESP provides with content protection and ensures that there is integrity and confidentiality for the message. IKE is used to make sure that only the intended sender and receiver can access the message.

questions.question: ATM standard defines _______ layers.
questions.explanation: The three layers are physical layer, ATM layer and application adoption layer. The physical layer corresponds to the physical layer, ATM layer corresponds to the data link layer and the AAL layer corresponds to the network layer of the OSI model.

questions.question: Propagation delay depends on ___________
questions.explanation: Propagation delay is caused when the packet is in its electric signal form and is travelling through a medium (a wire or a electromagnetic wave). Propagation delay is the time it takes a bit to propagate from one router to the next. If the distance between the routers is increased, it will take longer time to propagate, that is, there would be more propagation delay.

questions.question: Which protocols are used for Storage management?
questions.explanation: Simple Network Management Protocol is used for storage management. Lightweight Directory Access Protocol is used to access or locate information about directories and other resources on a network. Post Office Protocol 3 is used for e-mailing on the internet. Management Information Base is a part of SNMP and contains hierarchically organized information.

questions.question: Which standard TCP port is assigned for contacting SSH servers?
questions.explanation: Port 22 is used for contacting ssh servers, used for file transfers (scp, sftp) and also port forwarding.

questions.question: In data transfer phase, how many columns does the table contain?
questions.explanation: The switch maintains a table for each Virtual Circuit Network. In the data transfer phase, it maintains 2 columns each for incoming data and outgoing data. The columns are in the following order: Source port, Source VCI, Destination port, Destination VCI.

questions.question: What does the 'IPX maximum-paths 2' command accomplish?
options: It enables load sharing on 2 paths if the paths are equal metric paths
It sets up routing to go to network 2
It is the default for Cisco IPX load sharing
It enables load sharing on 2 paths if the paths are unequal metric paths
questions.explanation: It enables load sharing on 2 paths if the paths are equal metric paths. The default is 1 path and the maximum is 512 paths. The value must always be greater than 1 and must be a natural number.

questions.question: What is a Scatternet?
options: It is a group of interconnected Micronets
It is a group of interconnected Mininets
It is a group of interconnected Intranets
It is a group of interconnected Piconets
questions.explanation: Scatternet is a network connecting two or more Piconets having Bluetooth technology incorporated in their network devices. It supports communication between more than 8 devices. Thus, Scatternet can also be defined as interconnected Piconets.

questions.question: The RMON1 MIB groups and tables are used to gather statistics at the ________ in Ethernet and token ring LANs.
questions.explanation: The RMON1 MIB groups and tables are used to gather statistics at the data link layer in Ethernet and token ring LANs. The RMON2 MIB groups and tables define parameters for higher-layer statistics.

questions.question: Which of the following allows LAN users to share computer programs and data?
questions.explanation: A file server allows LAN users to share computer programs and data. It uses the File Transfer Protocol to provide this feature on ports 20 and 21. The file server works as a medium for the transfer.

questions.question: FDDI stands for _________
questions.explanation: Fiber Distributed Data Interface (FDDI) is a set of ANSI (American National Standards Institute) protocols. FDDI acts as a backbone network for WANs. It also provides high bandwidth for transmission of digital signal data through optical fiber cable.

questions.question: FTP uses _________ parallel TCP connections to transfer a file.
questions.explanation: Control connection using FTP port: 21, and data connection using FTP port: 20. The FTP session is started or ended using port 21 and the actual data i.e. files are sent through port 20.

questions.question: Which of the following is true with respect to the delay in datagram networks?
options: Delay is greater than in a virtual circuit network
Each packet may experience a wait at a switch
Delay is not uniform for the packets of a message
questions.explanation: The delay of each packet in a datagram network is different as each packet might take a different route to the destination. The delay includes the propagation delay and the processing delay that is induced at each stop/switch that the packet encounters in its journey.

questions.question: Typically the TCP port used by SMTP is _________
questions.explanation: The ports 15, 35 and 50 are all UDP ports and SMTP only uses TCP port 25 for reliability.

questions.question: Which of the following is true of signature-based IDSes?
options: They alert administrators to deviations from 'normal' traffic behavior
The technology is mature and reliable enough to use on production networks
They scan network traffic or packets to identify matches with attack-definition files
questions.explanation: They are constantly updated with attack-definition files (signatures) that describe each type of known malicious activity. They then scan network traffic for packets that match the signatures, and then raise alerts to security administrators.

questions.question: Which of the following is a disadvantage of symmetric cipher?
questions.explanation: Symmetric key cryptography uses single key to encrypt and decrypt messages. If the secret key is shared through an unsecured channel, then there will be a chance for attacking the key.

questions.question: ___________ device is used for connecting LAN.
questions.explanation: Bridges are also known as LAN Switches. Bridges are used for connecting two or more LANs. It also helps in forwarding the data packets to these networks.

questions.question: Task of Distributed Management Task Force is not ________
options: to promote interoperability among the management solution providers
to act as an interface between the various budding technologies and provide solution to manage various environments
to track the operation of the different management solution providers
to manage the facility by itself if one of the management solution providers fail
questions.explanation: The Distributed Management Task Force is used just to simplify the overall management of the network. It cannot manage a network facility by itself in case one of the management solution providers fails. It provides an interface for promoting interoperability among management solution providers.

questions.question: Which of the following can be used by a router to increase the processing speed of the packet?
questions.explanation: A router can contain a flow label table if it is capable of supporting flow labels. Flow label helps the router to find the next hop without consulting the routing table. If the router does not supports the flow label then it simply leaves it aside.

questions.question: There are ________ total features of Frame Relay.
questions.explanation: Frame relay is a wide area network technology used to transmit information over a network in the form of frames using relays. The frames are of variable size. It is cheaper than other WANs and it's simple to configure user equipment in the network.

questions.question: TMN had been developed based on ________ management principles.
questions.explanation: TMN is necessary to extend the private and proprietary, but well-developed network management systems, and make them interoperable. It is to be kept in mind that TMN had been developed based on OSI management principles.

questions.question: Which of the following device is a data link layer device?
questions.explanation: Switches are also called as modern bridges and uses the frame headers to transmit the frames to the destination computer.

questions.question: An email client needs to know the _________ of its initial SMTP server.
questions.explanation: The client needs to know the IP of its initial SMTP server as it has to send the mail first to that server and then the server forwards the mail ahead on behalf of the user.

questions.question: Which of the following cannot be an advantage of an access list?
options: It helps the network to perform well
It speeds up the packet transmission
It manages the traffic over the network
questions.explanation: An access list cannot speed up the packet transmission. An access list is nothing but a set of rules, which needs to be checked for a packet at the interface. So, the packet may take more time to be transmitted than usual time when it is subjected to checking.

questions.question: Which of the following is an application layer service?
questions.explanation: The services provided by the application layer are network virtual terminal, file transfer, access and management, mail services, directory services, various file and data operations.

questions.question: ________ is the best tool to gather network statistics.
questions.explanation: One of the best ways to gather network statistics is to capture packets traversing network segments or across node interfaces in a promiscuous mode. We have learned that protocol analyzers do just that. Thus, they are good tools to gather network statistics.

questions.question: An OSPF router receives an LSA. The router checks its sequence number and finds that this number is lower than the sequence number it already has. What does the router do with the LSA?
options: sends newer LSU update to source router
floods the LSA to the other routers
questions.explanation: An OSPF router receives an LSA. If the router checks its sequence number and finds that this number is lower than the sequence number that it already has, the router sends newer LSU update to source router. The router then adds it to the database and floods it to the other routers.

questions.question: Expansion of SMTP is ________
questions.explanation: SMTP or Simple Mail Transfer Protocol is an application layer protocol used to transport e-mails over the Internet. Only 7-bit ASCII codes can be sent using SMTP.

questions.question: Port number used by Network Time Protocol (NTP) with UDP is ________
questions.explanation: The Network Time Protocol is a clock synchronization network protocol implemented by using UDP port number 123 to send and receive time stamps.

questions.question: What does administrative distance rank?
questions.explanation: Sources of routing information is the administrative distance rank. It is used by routers to select the best path when there are different routes to the same destination. It's used only two different routing protocols are being used.

questions.question: If you have to send multimedia data over SMTP it has to be encoded into _______
questions.explanation: Since only 7-bit ASCII codes are transmitted through SMTP, it is mandatory to convert binary multimedia data to 7-bit ASCII before it is sent using SMTP.

questions.question: If a link transmits 4000 frames per second, and each slot has 8 bits, what is the transmission rate of the circuit using Time Division Multiplexing (TDM)?
questions.explanation: Transmission rate= frame rate * number of bits in a slot. Given: Frame rate = 4000/sec and number of bits in slot = 8 Thus, Transmission rate = (4000 * 8) bps = 32000bps = 32kbps

questions.question: WiMAX provides ________
questions.explanation: WiMax was developed to provide wireless broadband access to buildings. It can also be used to connect WLAN hotspots to the Internet. It is based on IEEE 802.16 standards.

questions.question: Which of the following is a disadvantage of the hierarchical routing?
questions.explanation: Hierarchical routing is simple but it is not optimum. In order to achieve optimum route, every router must be aware of all the routers in the network, it is not possible in hierarchical routing.

questions.question: A _____ is a TCP name for a transport service access point.
questions.explanation: Just as the IP address identifies the computer, the network port identifies the application or service running on the computer. A port number is 16 bits. The combination of IP address preceded with the port number is called the socket address.

questions.question: ICMP error message will not be generated for a datagram having a special address such as _______
questions.explanation: 127.0.0.0 is a special address known as the loopback address which is used for testing purpose of a machine without actually communicating with a network. Thus no error reporting message will be generated for such special addresses.

questions.question: Which of the following server is used to simulate the broadcast process while broadcast routing is being used?
questions.explanation: Broadcast unknown server will receive the broadcast frame and makes copies of it. And then, sends the copies to every station by initiating the process of broadcast routing.

questions.question: What is the significance of Textual convention?
options: It helps in defining new data types
It defines a minimum set of capabilities
It communicates with SNMPv2 manager over the UDP transport layer
It helps in improving the security function of SNMPv2
questions.explanation: Textual Conventions are designed to help define new data types. They are also intended to make the semantics consistent and clear for humans.

questions.question: What is one advantage of setting up a DMZ with two firewalls?
options: You can control where traffic goes in three networks
You can do stateful packet filtering
questions.explanation: DMZ stands for De-Militarized Zone. In a topology with a single firewall serving both internal and external users (LAN and WAN), it acts as a shared resource for these two zones. So load balancing can be done by adding another firewall.

questions.question: A firewall needs to be __________ so that it can grow proportionally with the network that it protects.
questions.explanation: The firewall has to be scalable because a network is expected to grow with time and if the firewall is unable to grow with it, the firewall won"t be able to handle the growing network traffic flow and will hence fail.

questions.question: Router belongs to which layer?
questions.explanation: Router belongs to network layer in OSI architecture. Routers are mainly used for packet switching between networks. Bridge and switch are data link layer devices. Repeater and hub are physical layer devices. Application gateway is used in application layer.

questions.question: In OSPF database descriptor packet, which field is used to detect a missing packet?
questions.explanation: Sequence number field is used to detect a missing packet. The packets are to be received in order of the sequence number, so if the receiver detects that there is a sequence number skipped or missing in the order, it stops processing the further received packets and informs the sender to retransmit the packets in sequence.

questions.question: What protocol is NOT used in the operation of a VPN?
questions.explanation: PPTP is a tunneling protocol which was initially used for the creation of VPNs. IPSec is used in encrypting the traffic flowing in the VPN. L2TP is used to tunnel all the L2 traffic on the VPN.

questions.question: RMON probe is a type of probe measures traffic at the cell _________ level only.
questions.explanation: The RMON probe is an embedded probe with access to the switch fabric. However, this type of probe measures traffic at the cell header level only.

questions.question: Bluetooth is the wireless technology for __________
questions.explanation: Bluetooth is a wireless technology used to create a wireless personal area network for data transfer up to a distance of 10 meters. It operates on 2.45 GHz frequency band for transmission.

questions.question: How a path can be defined in border gateway protocol?
questions.explanation: When traffic is sent from one network to another network, then every router within the route checks the path. This path contains not only the number of AS but also contains attributes that give information about the path such as next_hop.

questions.question: Which of the following type of multiple access protocols uses a scheme called "Authorization from other stations"?
questions.explanation: In controlled access protocols, stations send frames only if they get the authorization from the other stations to use the channel. This authorization can be obtained using methods such as reservation, polling, token passing.

questions.question: _________ is the default administrative distance of RIP.
questions.explanation: The default administrative distance is the default count of numbers assigned to arbitrary routes to a destination. The default administrative distance of RIP is 120. It is used to find the shortest route amongst the number of paths available.

questions.question: FTP server listens for connection on which port number?
questions.explanation: FTP server listens on port 21 for connection request whereas port 20 is used for FTP data transfer. Port 22 is used for SSH remote login. Port 23 is used for TELNET.

questions.question: Which of the following is used in an attempt to render a computer resource unavailable to its intended users?
questions.explanation: In a Denial of Service attack, the attacker won"t let the victims access the network by using a certain method that ensures that an essential network resource is unavailable to the victim. The methods that the attacker can use are vulnerability attack, bandwidth flooding and connection flooding.

questions.question: How many bits are required to represent a virtual local area network identifier in an ethernet frame?
questions.explanation: VLAN identifier occupies 12 bits in a frame tag to be distinguished from other VLANs. A tag field is present in between a source MAC address and ethernet type in the ethernet frame. This tag contains VLAN identifier.

questions.question: The process where a tool "sniffs" every packet that is going across a local area network (LAN), opens it and analyzes it is a __________ operation.
questions.explanation: SNMP messages going across the network between a manager and an agent is examined. This is accomplished by using a tool that "sniffs" every packet that is going across a local area network (LAN), opens it and analyzes it. Thus it is a passive operation.

questions.question: The main difference between SNMPv3 and SNMPv2 is _______
questions.explanation: SNMPv3 has introduced new cryptographic security, through which confidentiality is provided by encrypting packets and blocking intruders. It also ensures that the message is coming from a reliable source.

questions.question: What is the function of an SNMP Sniff tool?
options: It captures SNMP packets going across the segment and stores them for later analysis
It displays the entire MIB tree supported by the agent
It is useful to get the values of variables in table
It sends the SNMP set-request message and receives the get-response command
questions.explanation: The SNMP Sniff Tool snmpsniff; is similar to the tcpdump tool and is implemented in Linux/FreeBSD environment. It captures SNMP packets going across the segment and stores them for later analysis.

questions.question: An SNMP _______ is a collection of management information accessible by an SNMP entity.
questions.explanation: There are five elements of VACM model. They are: (I) groups, (2) security level (3) contexts, (4) MIB views and view families, and (5) access policy. An SNMP context is a collection of management information accessible by an SNMP entity. An SNMP entity has access to potentially more than one context. Each SNMP engine has a context table that lists the locally available contexts by contextName.

questions.question: Internet works on _______
options: both packet switching and circuit switching
questions.explanation: Packet switching is the method based on which the internet works. Packet switching features delivery of packets of data between devices over a shared network.

questions.question: In asynchronous serial communication the physical layer provides ___________
options: both start & stop signalling and flow control
questions.explanation: In asynchronous serial communication, the communication is not synchronized by clock signal. Instead of a start and stop signaling and flow control method is followed. Unlike asynchronous serial communication, in synchronous serial communication a clock signal is used for communication, so the start and stop method is not really required.

questions.question: A family of network control protocols (NCPs) ____________
options: Are a series of independently defined protocols that provide a dynamic
Are a series of independently-defined protocols that encapsulate
Are a series of independently defined protocols that provide transparent
questions.explanation: The family of network control protocols (NCPs) is a series of independently-defined protocols that encapsulate the data flowing between the two nodes. It provides means for the network nodes to control the link traffic.

questions.question: What is the other name for key hash function, which is used in message authentication?
questions.explanation: Message authentication code that is key hash function is mostly used in message authentication. This MAC function results a MAC value, by taking the input block of data and secret key. This MAC value is used to check whether data has changed or not.

questions.question: If link transmits 4000frames per second, and each slot has 8 bits, the transmission rate of circuit this TDM is _________
questions.explanation: Transmission rate= frame rate * number of bits in a slot. Given: Frame rate = 4000/sec and number of bits in slot = 8 Thus, Transmission rate = (4000 * 8) bps = 32000bps = 32kbps

questions.question: Which statement(s) about IPv6 addresses are true?
options: Two colons (::) are used to represent successive hexadecimal fields of zeros
Two colons (::) are used to separate fields
A single interface cannot have multiple IPv6 addresses of different types
questions.explanation: In order to shorten the written length of an IPv6 address, successive fields of zeros may be replaced by double colons. In trying to shorten the address further, leading zeros may also be removed. Just as with IPv4, a single device's interface can have more than one address; with IPv6 there are more types of addresses and the same rule applies. There can be link-local, global unicast, and multicast addresses all assigned to the same interface.

questions.question: The method for taking advantage of optical wavelength of transmission, in order to increase capacity of information in an optical transmission medium is known as __________
questions.explanation: Wavelength Division Multiplexing (WDM) is a multiplexing technique that combines a number of optical signals into a single optical fiber. It combines wavelength of these different optical carrier signals and helps in increasing the capacity of optical fibers by decreasing the transmission loss.

questions.question: A few leftmost bits in each address of IPv6 address define its category is called ________
questions.explanation: Prefix is the bits in the IP address which are placed in leftmost position. A network prefix in IPv6 is given by a CIDR format-liked number at the end of the address.

questions.question: What is the primary use of MAC filtering on wireless networks?
questions.explanation: MAC filtering allows only approved MAC addresses to send traffic in the network. It provides security, even if hacker hijacks the IP address. Because, the hacker will not be allowed into network without approved MAC address.

questions.question: Delimiting and synchronization of data exchange is provided by __________
questions.explanation: The session layer provides the mechanism for opening, closing and managing a session between end-user application processes. The session layer 5 is responsible for establishing managing synchronizing and terminating sessions. In TCP/IP protocol stack, the functions of the session layer are handled by the transport layer itself and thus the session layer is missing from the TCP/IP model.

questions.question: When were VPNs introduced into the commercial world?
questions.explanation: VPNs were first introduced in the year 1996. Then as the internet started to get popularized, the need for connection security increased. VPN was a great solution to this, and that's when VPNs were implemented in the commercial world.

questions.question: In wireless distribution system __________
options: multiple access point are inter-connected with each other
questions.explanation: A Wireless Distribution System allows the connection of multiple access points together. It is used to expand a wireless network to a larger network.

questions.question: If there are N routers from source to destination, the total end to end delay in sending packet P(L-> number of bits in the packet R-> transmission rate) is equal to ______________
questions.explanation: The equation to find the end to end delay when no. of bits, transmission rate and no. of routers is given by (N*L)/R. The total end to end delay, that is, nodal delay is the sum of all, the processing delay, queuing delay, transmission delay and propagation delay.

questions.question: Backpressure technique can be applied only to _______
questions.explanation: In Virtual circuit networks, each node knows the upstream node from which a flow data is coming. So, it makes possible for the congested node to track the source of the congestion and then inform that node to reduce the flow to remove congestion.

questions.question: In the transfer of file between server and client, if the transmission rates along the path is 10Mbps, 20Mbps, 30Mbps, 40Mbps. The throughput is usually ___________
questions.explanation: The throughput is generally the transmission rate of bottleneck link.

questions.question: Determine the number of valid host IPs in each subnet for the given IP 172.16.0.0/25 using the CIDR value.
questions.explanation: Given, CIDR value = 25 IP address = 172.16.0.0 (Class B) Total number of bits in an IP address = 32 Let n be the number of bits in the host portion of the given IP address n = Total number of bits in an IP address ' CIDR value n = 32 ' 25 n = 7 Number of Hosts = 2n ' 2 = 27 ' 2 Number of Hosts = 128 -2 Number of Hosts = 126 Since Network IP and Broadcast IP cannot be assigned to any host we remove those 2 IPs.

questions.question: Pretty good privacy (PGP) is used in ______
questions.explanation: PGP is an encryption method used in e-mail security to encrypt and decrypt the content of an e-mail transmitted over the internet. It makes sure that the message cannot be stolen by other unauthorized users.

questions.question: RMON1 dealt primarily with data associated with the OSI ______ layer.
questions.explanation: RMON1 is covered by RFC 1757 for Ethernet LAN and RFC 1513. RMON in general and RMON1 specifically, performs numerous functions at the data link layer. Hence it can be said that RMON1 majorly dealt with data that associated with data-link layer of the OSI model.

questions.question: Which of the following is not a transition strategy?
questions.explanation: As IPv4 addresses are of 32 bits and IPv6 addresses are of 128 bits, it is not possible to convert IPv4 address to IPv6 address. So, Dual stack, tunneling and header translation are the three strategies which might help in the transition from IPv4 to IPv6.

questions.question: In IPv6 addresses, addresses that start with eight 0s are called ________
questions.explanation: In IPv6 address format, the starting bits are specified with eight 0s to represent reserved addresses. These reserved addresses have a certain function pre-defined like the loop-back address is used to test a network card. Reserved addresses cannot be allotted to a machine.

questions.question: A wireless network interface controller can work in _______
options: both infrastructure mode and ad-hoc mode
questions.explanation: A wireless network interface controller works on the physical layer and the data link layer of the OSI model. Infrastructure mode WNIC needs access point but in ad-hoc mode access point is not required.

questions.question: What is the size of MAC address in medium access control sub layer?
questions.explanation: MAC address is a 48 bit with 6 octets and can be represented as EUI-48. As MAC address is a 48 bit space, 248 MAC addresses are possible. Non-Ethernet applications can have EUI-64 address.

questions.question: The domain name system is maintained by _______
questions.explanation: A domain name system is maintained by a distributed database system. It is a collection of multiple, logically interrelated databases distributed over a computer network.

questions.question: An ATM cell has the payload field of __________
questions.explanation: An ATM field contains a header and a payload. The header is of 5 bytes and the payload is of 48 bytes. The size of the header remains fixed.

questions.question: IP assigned for a client by DHCP server is
questions.explanation: The IP address offered to a client is only for a limited period of time. There is actually a certain amount of time that the client can use and keep this IP address.

questions.question: Integrated Services Digital Network (ISDN) is a ___________ interface between the subscriber and the central office.
questions.explanation: ISDN provides a set of standards for maintaining different call states. It is a circuit-switched telephone network system. It acts as an interface, and only a single device can be connected to this interface. This interface is based on full-duplex mode of data transmission.

questions.question: ATM and frame relay are ________
questions.explanation: ATM and frame relay are transmission modes in which information is transferred through electric circuit layer as packets. ATM has fixed packet size and frame relay has variable packet size.

questions.question: Token Ring and FDDI configurations use the _________ topology.
questions.explanation: FDDI is used for combining the Token Ring, Fast Ethernet and Fiber Optic Cable. Thus FDDI acts as a network backbone for connecting LANs to create MANs and WANs. FDDI uses dual-ring topology for this purpose instead of other network topologies.

questions.question: Which of the following cannot be achieved by the digital signature in symmetric key encryption?
questions.explanation: Channel security is not in hands of digital signature, even the secret key is being transmitted between sender and receiver. Security of the message will be depending on the channel that is being used.

questions.question: Which one of the following is not an application layer protocol?
questions.explanation: Resource reservation protocol is used in transport layer. It is designed to reserve resources across a network for quality of service using the integrated services model.

questions.question: What is the vulnerable time of the pure aloha for fixed length of frames (T=frame time)?
questions.explanation: Vulnerable time is time in which, there is a possibility for a collision. T(frame) is the time taken by a frame to be sent to destination. Vulnerable time will be 2 times the fixed length of frames.

questions.question: Which port is used for sending messages using SMTP securely?
questions.explanation: Port number 465 is used for sending messages using SMTP securely. Port 110 and Port 995 are used by POP3 and Port 143 is the default port number for IMAP.

questions.question: ________ of individual hosts are useful for identifying some hidden costs.
questions.explanation: Accounting management is probably the least developed function of the network management application. Accounting management could also include the use of individual hosts, administrative segments, and external traffic. Accounting of individual hosts is useful for identifying some hidden costs.

questions.question: Transmission delay does not depend on _____________
questions.explanation: Transmission delay = packet length / transmission rate. The transmission rate depends upon the bandwidth of the medium.

questions.question: Which of the following is not an application layer service?
questions.explanation: Application layer is the topmost layer in the OSI model. Network virtual terminal, mail service, file transfer, access and management are all services of the application layer. It uses protocols like HTTP, FTP, and DNS etc. to provide these services.

questions.question: The HTTP response message leaves out the requested object when ____________ method is used
questions.explanation: HEAD method is much faster than GET method. In HEAD method much smaller amount of data is transferred. The HEAD method asks only for information about a document and not for the document itself.

questions.question: What are the Methods to move data through a network of links and switches?
questions.explanation: Packet switching and Circuit switching are two different types of switching methods used to connect the multiple communicating devices with one another. Packet switching is used in conventional LAN systems and circuit switching is used in telephonic systems.

questions.question: You need to subnet a network into 5 subnets, each with at least 16 hosts. Which classful subnet mask would you use?
questions.explanation: You need 5 subnets, each with at least 16 hosts. The mask 255.255.255.240 provides 16 subnets with 14 hosts which is less than 15, so this will not work. The mask 255.255.255.224 provides 8 subnets, each with 30 hosts so this may work. The mask 255.255.255.192 provides 4 subnets, each with 60 hosts so this may work. Comparing both the possible masks, 255.255.255.224 provides the best answer.

questions.question: Which connection is necessary for a computer to join the internet?
questions.explanation: The ISPs (Internet Service Providers) are the main agents through which every computer is connected to the internet. They are licensed to allot public IP addresses to its customers in order to connect them to the internet.

questions.question: What is autonegotiation?
options: a procedure by which two connected devices choose common transmission parameters
questions.explanation: autonegotiation is a procedure by which two connected devices choose common transmission parameters. It is a signaling mechanism used in Ethernet over Twisted pair cables.

questions.question: Which NetWare protocol works on layer 3'network layer"of the OSI model?
questions.explanation: IPX (Internetwork Packet Exchange) is the NetWare network layer 3 protocol used for transferring information on LANs that use Novell's NetWare.

questions.question: Internet was developed from _______ with the advent of UNIX and the popularization of client-server architecture.
questions.explanation: ARPANET was developed into Internet. In 1984 the name ARPANET was dropped and the name "Internet" was adopted. It was developed after the arrival of UNIX and client-server architecture. Internet is limited to TCP/IP network.

questions.question: What is WPA?
questions.explanation: WPA or WiFi Protected Access is a security protocol used to provide users and firms with strong data security and protection for their wireless networks (WiFi) to give them confidence that only authorized users can access their network.

questions.question: A ___________ combines two autonomous networks.
questions.explanation: In an autonomous network, it is very important for the data to flow from one discrete network to the other and to convey the same meaning at every network. Thus, gateway is a network node component that helps in translating one protocol to the other and connects two completely independent networks.

questions.question: Effective Storage management does not include __________
questions.explanation: Connection is the responsibility of the connection manager. Storage management includes management of all necessities such as security, backups and reporting facilities.

questions.question: You type debug IP rip on your router console and see that 172.16.10.0 is being advertised to you with a metric of 16. What does this mean?
options: The route is 16 hops away Debug IP rip
The route has a delay of 16 microseconds Debug IP route
The route is queued at 16 messages a second
questions.explanation: You cannot have 16 hops on a RIP network by default, because the max default hops possible is 15. If you receive a route advertised with a metric of 16, this means it is inaccessible.

questions.question: In a network topology, when a data signal is transmitted, there are chances that this signal may bounce back to the device that has already carried the signal. In order to avoid this, we must use _____ at the end.
questions.explanation: A terminator is a device attached to the end of a bus in a network topology. The main objective of terminator is to absorb signals in order to avoid the signal to reflect back. Signal reflections produce interference that causes data loss.

questions.question: SSH-2 does not contain ______________
questions.explanation: SSH2 is a more secure, portable and efficient version of SSH that includes SFTP, which is functionally similar to FTP, but is SSH2 encrypted.

questions.question: Cryptographic hash function takes an arbitrary block of data and returns _________
options: both fixed size bit string and variable size bit string
questions.explanation: Cryptographic hash functions are used in digital signatures and message authentication codes. The only issue with it is that it returns the same hash value every time for a message making it vulnerable to attackers to evaluate and break the cipher.

questions.question: When is it prominent to use border gateway protocol?
options: When AS is not aware of path selection
When autonomous system is stab AS
when autonomous system is transit AS
questions.explanation: Border gateway protocol is more useful when the AS is transit because BGP is capable of inter-domain routing. So, it can handle the data traffic well when the ASs are transit.

questions.question: __________ is a routing technique deployed in the Edge Routers.
questions.explanation: The name edge router arises from the fact that edge routers generally operates on the edge of a network. MPLS is a routing technique that is used by Edge routers and these routers are termed as MPLS Edge routers. Whereas Fixed weighted, Round Robin and Adaptive balancing are load balancing techniques and not routing techniques.

questions.question: __________ is an example of Wide Area Network (WAN).
questions.explanation: Wide Area Networks (WAN) covers networks of large geographical areas. Since the internet is a phenomenon that is used throughout the world. Thus Internet access is an application of WAN. Whereas home router, cell networks, and cable TV networks are applications of LAN, PAN and MAN respectively.

questions.question: The conformance information, which encompasses both compliance and agent capabilities, is covered in _________
questions.explanation: The information on SNMPv2 has been split into three parts. MIB modules for SNMv2 are covered in RFC 1902. The textual conventions to be used to describe MIB modules have been formalized in RFC 1903. RFC 1904 defines SNMPv2 conformance statements for the implementation of network management standards.

questions.question: LSP stands for __________
questions.explanation: A Link State Packet is a packet created by a router that lists its neighboring nodes and routers in link state routing protocol. It is shared with other routers to find the shortest path from a source to the destination.

questions.question: State whether true or false. i) A connection oriented protocol can only use unicast addresses. ii) The any cast service is included in IPV6.
questions.explanation: In a connection oriented protocol, the host can only establish connection with another host on one unique channel, that's why it can only use unicast addresses. In IPv6, there is an anycast address in IPv6 which allows sending messages to a group of devices but not all devices in a network.

questions.question: The server program tells its TCP that it is ready to accept a connection. This process is called ___________
questions.explanation: This is the first step in the Three-Way Handshaking process and is started by the server. Then the Client picks an ISN (Initial Sequence Number) and synchronizes (shares) it with the Server requesting a connection. The Server acknowledges the clients ISN, and then picks an ISN and synchronizes it with the Client. At last, the Client acknowledges the servers ISN.

questions.question: Which of the following is not a well-known mandatory attribute of path in border gateway protocol?
questions.explanation: Origin defines the source of the packet and As_path defines the sequence of ASs that a packet follows and Next_hop defines about the next router that a packet is going to visit.

questions.question: In case of time exceeded error, when the datagram visits a router, the value of time to live field is _________
questions.explanation: This field will be decremented by 1 at every router, and will be zero by the time it reaches source. This error reporting message is type 11 and is used to prevent the router from travelling forever in case some unknown path anomaly occurs.

questions.question: "Total length" field in UDP packet header is the length of _________
questions.explanation: Total length is the 16 bit field which contains the length of UDP header and the data. The maximum value of the Total length field and the maximum size of a UDP datagram is 65,535 bytes (8 byte header + 65,527 bytes of data).

questions.question: What is the central device in star topology?
questions.explanation: In star topology, no computer is connected to another computer directly but all the computers are connected to a central switch or hub. Every message sent from a source computer goes through the switch or hub and the switch or hub then forwards the message only to the intended destination computer.

questions.question: Home Internet Access is provided by __________
questions.explanation: Home Internet Access is provided by DSL, FTTP, and Cable. FTTP provides the fastest speeds followed by the cable connections and then the DSLs. FTTP is popularly used in modern connections.

questions.question: What does the following series of commands "Router IGRP 71 network" accomplish? 10.0.0.0 router IGRP 109 network 172.68.7.0
options: It loads IGRP for networks 109 and 71
questions.explanation: It isolates network 10.0.0.0 and 172.68.7.0 and associates autonomous systems 109 and71 with IGRP. IGRP does not disable RIP, both can be used at the same time.

questions.question: After obtaining the IP address, to prevent the IP conflict the client may use _________
questions.explanation: ARP abbreviation for address resolution protocol is used for mapping IP addresses to MAC addresses that are present in the local network.

questions.question: Which one of the following multimedia formats can not be supported by RTP?
questions.explanation: RTP is suitable only for multimedia and not for simple text files as the operation would result into wastage of resources. Other protocols like FTP are suitable for such transmissions.

questions.question: Application layer protocol defines ____________
options: rules for when and how processes send and respond to messages
questions.explanation: Application layer deals with the user interface, what message is to be sent or the message format, syntax and semantics. A user has access to application layer for sending and receiving messages.

questions.question: Which of the following is not related to ipconfig in Microsoft Windows?
options: Display all current TCP/IP network configuration values
Trace the routers in the path to destination
questions.explanation: The Tracert command is available on Microsoft Windows to find the path i.e. the number of the routers that the packet has to go through to reach its destination.

questions.question: What is on-demand mail relay (ODMR)?
questions.explanation: ODMR is an extension to SMTP, in which mails are relayed to the receivers after they are authenticated. It allows only the authorized receivers to receive the mail.

questions.question: What is route poisoning?
options: It sends back the protocol received from a router as a poison pill, which stops the regular updates. The use of variable length subnet masks is permitted
It is information received from a router that can't be sent back to the originating router.RIPv2 supports classless routing
It prevents regular update messages from reinstating a route that has just come up
It describes when a router sets the metric for a downed link to infinity
questions.explanation: When a network goes down, the distance-vector routing protocol initiates route poisoning by advertising the network with a metric of 16, or unreachable.

questions.question: The path layer of SONET is responsible for the movement of a signal _________
options: from its optical source to its optical destination
questions.explanation: The path layer in SONET is responsible for the movement signal from the optical source to the optical destination. It is ideally expected to find the shortest and the most reliable path to the destination.

questions.question: The __________ portion of LAN management software restricts access, records user activities and audit data, etc.
questions.explanation: The Security management portion of LAN management software restricts access, records user activities, and audit data. It is responsible for controlling access to the network based on predefined policy. The security management ensures authentication, confidentiality, and integrity in the LAN.

questions.question: ICMP is primarily used for __________
questions.explanation: ICMP abbreviation for Internet Control Message Protocol is used by networking devices to send error messages and operational information indicating a host or router cannot be reached. ICMP operates over the IP packet to provide error reporting functionality as IP by itself cannot report errors.

questions.question: In the SNMP architecture ____________ is a system that collects and transmits local device information.
questions.explanation: SNMP architecture comprises of Manager, Agent and Network Management station. A SNMP agent does the function of responding to requests and queries and also trapping problems. It also collects and transmits device information.

questions.question: 100BaseT4 networks are wired together using ____________
questions.explanation: 100BaseT4 is one of the three types of Ethernet cable. These physical wiring can carry up to 100 Mbps. 100BaseT4 networks uses Star topology for its connections.

questions.question: Which of the following is a Boolean cookie attribute?
questions.explanation: The final cookie attribute is a boolean attribute named secure that specifies how cookie values are transmitted over the network. By default, cookies are insecure, which means that they are transmitted over a normal, insecure HTTP connection. If a cookie is marked secure, however, it is transmitted only when the browser and server are connected via HTTPS or another secure protocol.

questions.question: Delay of the resource allocated on demand during data transfer is ________
questions.explanation: If a resource is to be allocated on demand during the data transfer phase, the delay for each packet would be different depending upon the resource requirement of the packets.

questions.question: To use a Simple Network Management System, we need _______
questions.explanation: Rules are a collection of expression containing parameters to observe the attributes of the user's device, and then execute some actions. It specifies the parameters for the managed objects inside the application and performs operations that would support the expression. The input of a rule may be many expressions or even a single expression that end in an output of single object invoking some action.

questions.question: Which of the following HTTP Status code means CREATED, when a resource is successful created using POST or PUT request?
questions.explanation: HTTP Status Code 201 means CREATED, when a resource is successful created using POST or PUT request. The code 200 means success i.e. OK, code 204 means NO CONTENT, and the code 304 means NOT MODIFIED.

questions.question: Which among the following features is present in IPv6 but not in IPv4?
questions.explanation: There is an anycast address in IPv6 which allows sending messages to a group of devices but not all devices in a network. Anycast address is not standardized in IPv4.

questions.question: Which of the following groups can generate a report of the top N protocol conversation?
questions.explanation: The application layer functions are grouped into two groups, the application-layer host group and the application layer matrix group. They both calculate traffic by protocol units and use their respective control tables in the network-layer host group and the network-layer matrix group. The application-layer matrix group can also generate a report of the top N protocol conversations.

questions.question: The structure or format of data is called ___________
questions.explanation: The structure and format of data are defined using syntax. Semantics defines how a particular pattern to be interpreted, and what action is to be taken based on that interpretation. In programming languages, syntax of the instructions plays a vital role in designing of the program.

questions.question: Which of the following protocol(s) supports uni-casting?
questions.explanation: TCP supports uni-casting transmission, where only one receiver and one sender will be present and it requires source and destination IP addresses only. Multi-casting is another version of transmission in which more than one receiver can be present.

questions.question: The source-quench message in ICMP was designed to add a kind of ____________ to the IP.
